subject: Language Arts
description: Use words and phrases acquired through conversations, reading and being read to, and responding to texts, including using frequently occurring conjunctions to signal simple relationships (e.g., because).
description_plain: Your child can use new words and phrases learned from talking, reading, and listening, including words like 'because' to show simple ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among verbs differing in manner (e.g., look, peek, glance, stare, glare, scowl) and adjectives differing in intensity (e.g., large, gigantic) by defining or choosing them or by acting out the meanings.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between similar action words and describing words by explaining, choosing, or acting them out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use (e.g., note places at home that are cozy).
description_plain: Your child can connect words to real-life things, like noticing cozy places at home.

subject: Language Arts
description: Define words by category and by one or more key attributes (e.g., a duck is a bird that swims; a tiger is a large cat with stripes).
description_plain: Your child can explain words by their group and important features, like saying a duck is a bird that swims.

subject: Language Arts
description: Sort words into categories (e.g., colors, clothing) to gain a sense of the concepts the categories represent.
description_plain: Your child can sort words into groups like colors or clothes to understand what those groups mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: With help, your child can understand how words relate to each other and different meanings of words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify frequently occurring root words (e.g., look) and their inflectional forms (e.g., looks, looked, looking).
description_plain: Your child can recognize common root words and their different forms, like 'look,' 'looks,' and 'looking.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring affixes as a clue to the meaning of a word.
description_plain: Your child can use common beginnings and endings of words to help figure out what the words mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use sentence-level context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from sentences to understand the meaning of words or phrases.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 1 reading and content, choosing flexibly from an array of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words using different strategies.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell untaught words phonetically, drawing on phonemic awareness and spelling conventions.
description_plain: Your child can spell new words by sounding them out and using spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use conventional spelling for words with common spelling patterns and for frequently occurring irregular words.
description_plain: Your child can spell common words correctly, including some irregular words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas in dates and to separate single words in a series.
description_plain: Your child can use commas in dates and to separate words in a list.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use end punctuation for sentences.
description_plain: Your child can use punctuation marks at the end of sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize dates and names of people.
description_plain: Your child can capitalize names of people and dates.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce and expand complete simple and compound declarative, interrogative, imperative, and exclamatory sentences in response to prompts.
description_plain: Your child can make and expand complete sentences that tell, ask, command, or show strong feelings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring prepositions (e.g., during, beyond, toward).
description_plain: Your child can use common words that show location or time, like 'during' or 'toward.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use determiners (e.g., articles, demonstratives).
description_plain: Your child can use words like 'a,' 'the,' and 'this' to point out things.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring conjunctions (e.g., and, but, or, so, because).
description_plain: Your child can use common connecting words like 'and,' 'but,' and 'because.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring adjectives.
description_plain: Your child can use describing words to add details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use verbs to convey a sense of past, present, and future (e.g., Yesterday I walked home; Today I walk home; Tomorrow I will walk home).
description_plain: Your child can use verbs to talk about things that happened before, now, or will happen later.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use personal, possessive, and indefinite pronouns (e.g., I, me, my; they, them, their; anyone, everything).
description_plain: Your child can use words like I, me, my, they, them, their, anyone, and everything correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use singular and plural nouns with matching verbs in basic sentences (e.g., He hops; We hop).
description_plain: Your child can use singular and plural nouns with matching verbs in simple sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, proper, and possessive nouns.
description_plain: Your child can use common nouns, names of people or places, and show possession.

subject: Language Arts
description: Print all upper- and lowercase letters.
description_plain: Your child can print all uppercase and lowercase letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and sentence structure when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses a wide range of academic and subject-specific words for reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and can find out new word meanings on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of unknown or tricky words by using clues from the sentence, breaking the word into parts, or looking them up in dictionaries or other resources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about language to understand how it works in different situations and to choose words and styles that fit what they want to say or understand better when reading or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and word usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce complete sentences when appropriate to task and situation.
description_plain: Your child will say complete sentences when it fits the situation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add drawings or other visual displays to descriptions when appropriate to clarify ideas, thoughts, and feelings.
description_plain: Your child can add drawings or pictures to help explain their ideas and feelings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe people, places, things, and events with relevant details, expressing ideas and feelings clearly.
description_plain: Your child describes people, places, things, and events with clear details and feelings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about what a speaker says in order to gather additional information or clarify something that is not understood.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions to understand what someone else is saying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about key details in a text read aloud or information presented orally or through other media.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions about important details in stories or information they hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask questions to clear up any confusion about the topics and texts under discussion.
description_plain: Your child asks questions to clear up confusion about topics or stories being discussed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Build on others' talk in conversations by responding to the comments of others through multiple exchanges.
description_plain: Your child listens and responds to others during conversations, having back-and-forth talks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions (e.g., listening to others with care, speaking one at a time about the topics and texts under discussion).
description_plain: Your child follows simple rules for talking with others, like listening carefully and speaking one at a time.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in collaborative conversations with diverse partners about grade 1 topics and texts with peers and adults in small and larger groups
description_plain: Your child takes part in group talks with classmates and adults about first-grade topics and stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child changes how they speak depending on the situation and uses formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child uses digital tools and visuals to share information and make presentations easier to understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child shares information and evidence clearly so listeners can follow their ideas and the way they organize their talk fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child thinks about a speaker’s opinion, reasons, and evidence to decide how strong their argument is.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child combines and evaluates information from different sources like pictures, numbers, and spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child gets ready for and takes part in conversations with different people, building on others’ ideas and sharing their own clearly and confidently.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, recall information from experiences or gather information from provided sources to answer a question.
description_plain: With help, your child remembers or finds information to answer questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in shared research and writing projects (e.g., explore a number of "how-to" books on a given topic and use them to write a sequence of instructions).
description_plain: Your child works with others to research and write, like reading 'how-to' books and writing steps.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, use a variety of digital tools to produce and publish writing, including in collaboration with peers.
description_plain: With support, your child uses computers or tablets to write and share their work, sometimes with friends.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, focus on a topic, respond to questions and suggestions from peers, and add details to strengthen writing as needed.
description_plain: With help, your child focuses on a topic, listens to ideas from others, and adds details to improve their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives in which they recount two or more appropriately sequenced events, include some details regarding what happened, use temporal words to signal event order, and provide some sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child writes stories that tell about two or more events in order, with some details and an ending.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts in which they name a topic, supply some facts about the topic, and provide some sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child writes to explain a topic by naming it, giving some facts, and finishing with a closing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces in which they introduce the topic or name the book they are writing about, state an opinion, supply a reason for the opinion, and provide some sense of closure.
description_plain: Your child writes opinion pieces by stating a topic or book, giving their opinion with a reason, and ending the piece.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly over short and long periods for different tasks and audiences, including time to research and revise.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child will find and use evidence from stories or information texts to support their thinking and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from books and online sources, check if the information is trustworthy, and use it without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child will do short and longer research projects by asking questions and showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology like the Internet to write, publish, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child will improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to express ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organized for the purpose and audience they are writing for.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child will write stories about real or imagined events using good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain complex ideas clearly by choosing and organizing information well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child will write arguments that support their claims with good reasons and enough evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child uses clues from the story to figure out or fix words they don’t know, rereading if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level text out loud with accuracy, good speed, and expression after practicing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level text with a clear purpose and understanding.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child reads accurately and smoothly enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words.
description_plain: Your child recognizes and reads common words that don’t follow usual spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read words with inflectional endings.
description_plain: Your child reads words with endings that change their meaning, like -ed or -s.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode two-syllable words following basic patterns by breaking the words into syllables.
description_plain: Your child reads two-syllable words by breaking them into parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge that every syllable must have a vowel sound to determine the number of syllables in a printed word.
description_plain: Your child knows every syllable has a vowel sound and uses this to count syllables in words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know final -e and common vowel team conventions for representing long vowel sounds.
description_plain: Your child knows that a final -e or vowel teams make long vowel sounds in words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode regularly spelled one-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child reads regularly spelled one-syllable words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know the spelling-sound correspondences for common consonant digraphs.
description_plain: Your child knows common letter pairs that make one sound, like 'sh' or 'ch'.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child uses phonics and word skills to read new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Segment spoken single-syllable words into their complete sequence of individual sounds (phonemes).
description_plain: Your child can say all the individual sounds in a spoken one-syllable word.

subject: Language Arts
description: Isolate and pronounce initial, medial vowel, and final sounds (phonemes) in spoken single-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can say the first, middle vowel, and last sounds in a spoken one-syllable word.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orally produce single-syllable words by blending sounds (phonemes), including consonant blends.
description_plain: Your child can say a one-syllable word by putting together individual sounds, including blends.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish long from short vowel sounds in spoken single-syllable words.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between long and short vowel sounds in spoken one-syllable words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of spoken words, syllables, and sounds (phonemes).
description_plain: Your child understands spoken words, syllables, and sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize the distinguishing features of a sentence (e.g., first word, capitalization, ending punctuation).
description_plain: Your child recognizes what makes a sentence, like starting with a capital letter and ending with punctuation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of the organization and basic features of print.
description_plain: Your child understands how print works and the basic parts of reading and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, read informational texts appropriately complex for grade 1.
description_plain: With help, your child reads informational books that are right for first grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify basic similarities in and differences between two texts on the same topic (e.g., in illustrations, descriptions, or procedures).
description_plain: Your child can find simple similarities and differences between two texts about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the reasons an author gives to support points in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the reasons an author gives to support their points in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the illustrations and details in a text to describe its key ideas.
description_plain: Your child uses pictures and details in a text to explain its main ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish between information provided by pictures or other illustrations and information provided by the words in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between information shown in pictures and information written in the words of a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and use various text features (e.g., headings, tables of contents, glossaries, electronic menus, icons) to locate key facts or information in a text.
description_plain: Your child can use parts of a book like headings, tables of contents, and glossaries to find important facts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to help determine or clarify the meaning of words and phrases in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions to understand the meaning of words and phrases in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the connection between two individuals, events, ideas, or pieces of information in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how two people, events, or ideas in a text are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the main topic and retell key details of a text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main topic of a text and retell important details about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about important details in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, read prose and poetry of appropriate complexity for grade 1.
description_plain: With help, your child can read stories and poems that are right for first grade.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the adventures and experiences of characters in stories.
description_plain: Your child can compare what different characters do and experience in stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use illustrations and details in a story to describe its characters, setting, or events.
description_plain: Your child can use pictures and details in a story to describe the characters, where it happens, or what happens.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify who is telling the story at various points in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell who is telling the story at different parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain major differences between books that tell stories and books that give information, drawing on a wide reading of a range of text types.
description_plain: Your child can explain the main differences between storybooks and books that give information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify words and phrases in stories or poems that suggest feelings or appeal to the senses.
description_plain: Your child can find words in stories or poems that show feelings or describe what we see, hear, smell, taste, or touch.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe characters, settings, and major events in a story, using key details.
description_plain: Your child can describe characters, places, and important events in a story using details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Retell stories, including key details, and demonstrate understanding of their central message or lesson.
description_plain: Your child can retell stories with important details and explain the main message or lesson.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions about important details in a story or poem.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and information on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts talk about the same ideas or topics and explain the different ways the authors write about them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and think about the main points and reasons in a text and decide if the evidence is strong and relevant.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and judge information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the author's point of view or purpose affects what is written and how it is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can look at how sentences and paragraphs fit together in a text and explain how they help the whole story or article.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases in a text, including special or figurative meanings, and explain how word choices affect the feeling or meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas change and connect throughout a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize the important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what a text says and make logical guesses, using examples from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate conversational, general academic, and domain-specific words and phrases, including those that signal spatial and temporal relationships (e.g., After dinner that night we went looking for them).
description_plain: Your child can learn and use words that are right for third grade, including words that show time and place.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among related words that describe states of mind or degrees of certainty (e.g., knew, believed, suspected, heard, wondered).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between similar words that show how sure someone is or how they feel.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use (e.g., describe people who are friendly or helpful).
description_plain: Your child can connect words to real-life examples, like describing people who are friendly or helpful.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish the literal and nonliteral meanings of words and phrases in context (e.g., take steps).
description_plain: Your child can understand when words or phrases mean exactly what they say and when they mean something different.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child can understand how words are related and the small differences in their meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use glossaries or beginning dictionaries, both print and digital, to determine or clarify the precise meaning of key words and phrases.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries or glossaries, in books or online, to find the exact meaning of important words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a known root word as a clue to the meaning of an unknown word with the same root (e.g., company, companion).
description_plain: Your child can use a word they know to help figure out the meaning of a new word with the same root.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of the new word formed when a known affix is added to a known word (e.g., agreeable/disagreeable, comfortable/uncomfortable, care/careless, heat/preheat).
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of a new word by looking at the beginning or ending added to a word they know.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use sentence-level context as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use the sentence around a word to help understand what the word means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning word and phrases based on grade 3 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words by using different strategies while reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and observe differences between the conventions of spoken and written standard English.
description_plain: Your child can notice and use the differences between how people speak and how they write standard English.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose words and phrases for effect.
description_plain: Your child can choose words and phrases to make their writing or speaking more interesting.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials, including beginning dictionaries, as needed to check and correct spellings.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries or other reference books to check and fix their spelling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use spelling patterns and generalizations (e.g., word families, position-based spellings, syllable patterns, ending rules, meaningful word parts) in writing words.
description_plain: Your child can use spelling patterns and rules to help write words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use conventional spelling for high-frequency and other studied words and for adding suffixes to base words (e.g., sitting, smiled, cries, happiness).
description_plain: Your child can spell common words correctly and add endings like -ing or -ed the right way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use commas and quotation marks in dialogue.
description_plain: Your child can use commas and quotation marks correctly when writing conversations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize appropriate words in titles.
description_plain: Your child will capitalize the right words in titles, like book or movie names.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child understands how to use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce simple, compound, and complex sentences.
description_plain: Your child can write simple, compound, and complex sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use coordinating and subordinating conjunctions.
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'and,' 'but,' and 'because' to connect ideas in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use comparative and superlative adjectives and adverbs, and choose between them depending on what is to be modified.
description_plain: Your child can use words that compare things, like 'bigger' or 'biggest,' and knows when to use each.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ensure subject-verb and pronoun-antecedent agreement.
description_plain: Your child makes sure subjects and verbs agree and uses pronouns correctly with their nouns.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use the simple (e.g., I walked; I walk; I will walk) verb tenses.
description_plain: Your child can use past, present, and future verb tenses correctly, like 'walked,' 'walk,' and 'will walk.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use regular and irregular verbs.
description_plain: Your child knows how to use regular verbs and verbs that change in special ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use abstract nouns (e.g., childhood).
description_plain: Your child uses nouns that name ideas or feelings, like 'childhood' or 'happiness.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Form and use regular and irregular plural nouns.
description_plain: Your child can make nouns plural, including those that change in unusual ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the function of nouns, pronouns, verbs, adjectives, and adverbs in general and their functions in particular sentences.
description_plain: Your child understands what nouns, pronouns, verbs, adjectives, and adverbs do in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and word usage when speaking and writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses a wide range of academic and subject-specific words for reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and can find out new word meanings on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of unknown or tricky words by using clues from the sentence, breaking the word into parts, or looking them up in dictionaries or other resources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about language to understand how it works in different situations and to choose words and styles that fit what they want to say or understand better when reading or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and word usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speak in complete sentences when appropriate to task and situation in order to provide requested detail or clarification.
description_plain: Your child speaks in full sentences to give details or explain when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Create engaging audio recordings of stories or poems that demonstrate fluid reading at an understandable pace; add visual displays when appropriate to emphasize or enhance certain facts or details.
description_plain: Your child can make interesting audio recordings of stories or poems and add pictures to highlight important parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report on a topic or text, tell a story, or recount an experience with appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details, speaking clearly at an understandable pace.
description_plain: Your child tells about a topic, story, or experience clearly with facts and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about information from a speaker, offering appropriate elaboration and detail.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions about what someone else says, adding details when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main ideas and supporting details of a text read aloud or information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child finds the main ideas and important details from stories or information shared in different ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain their own ideas and understanding in light of the discussion.
description_plain: Your child explains their own ideas and what they understand during discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask questions to check understanding of information presented, stay on topic, and link their comments to the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child asks questions to understand better, stays on topic, and connects their comments to what others say.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions (e.g., gaining the floor in respectful ways, listening to others with care, speaking one at a time about the topics and texts under discussion).
description_plain: Your child follows rules for group talks, like waiting their turn and listening carefully.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation and other information known about the topic to explore ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions ready by reading or learning about the topic beforehand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 3 topics and texts, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child takes part in different kinds of group talks, sharing their ideas clearly and building on others' ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child changes how they speak depending on the situation and uses formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child uses digital tools and visuals to share information and make presentations easier to understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child shares information and evidence clearly so listeners can follow their ideas and the way they organize their talk fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child thinks about a speaker’s opinion, reasons, and evidence to decide how strong their argument is.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child combines and evaluates information from different sources like pictures, numbers, and spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child gets ready for and takes part in conversations with different people, building on others’ ideas and sharing their own clearly and confidently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly, sometimes over several days and sometimes in one sitting, for different reasons and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall information from experiences or gather information from print and digital sources; take brief notes on sources and sort evidence into provided categories.
description_plain: Your child remembers information from experiences or finds facts from books and websites, taking short notes and organizing them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects that build knowledge about a topic.
description_plain: Your child does short research projects to learn more about a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, use technology to produce and publish writing (using keyboarding skills) as well as to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: With help, your child uses computers or tablets to write, publish, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, and editing.
description_plain: With help from adults and friends, your child plans, revises, and edits their writing to make it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, produce writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task and purpose.
description_plain: With support, your child writes pieces that are organized and fit the purpose.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use temporal words and phrases to signal event order.
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'then' and 'after' to show the order of events in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use dialogue and descriptions of actions, thoughts, and feelings to develop experiences and events or show the response of characters to situations.
description_plain: Your child uses dialogue and descriptions of actions and feelings to show what happens and how characters react.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish a situation and introduce a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally.
description_plain: Your child starts stories by setting the scene and introducing characters, then tells events in a natural order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, descriptive details, and clear event sequences.
description_plain: Your child writes stories about real or imagined events using good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child writes a closing sentence or section to finish their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use linking words and phrases (e.g., also, another, and, more, but) to connect ideas within categories of information.
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'also,' 'and,' and 'but' to connect ideas in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, and details.
description_plain: Your child adds facts, definitions, and details to explain a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic and group related information together; include illustrations when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child introduces a topic and groups related information together, using pictures when helpful.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas and information clearly.
description_plain: Your child writes clear informative texts to explain a topic and share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section.
description_plain: Your child writes a closing sentence or section to finish their opinion writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use linking words and phrases (e.g., because, therefore, since, for example) to connect opinion and reasons.
description_plain: Your child uses words like 'because' and 'for example' to connect their opinions and reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide reasons that support the opinion.
description_plain: Your child can give reasons to support their opinion when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce the topic or text they are writing about, state an opinion, and create an organizational structure that lists reasons.
description_plain: Your child will introduce a topic, share their opinion, and organize their reasons clearly in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts, supporting a point of view with reasons.
description_plain: Your child can write opinion pieces that explain their point of view with supporting reasons.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly over short and long periods for different tasks and audiences, including time to research and revise.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child will find and use evidence from stories or information texts to support their thinking and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from books and online sources, check if the information is trustworthy, and use it without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child will do short and longer research projects by asking questions and showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology like the Internet to write, publish, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child will improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to express ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organized for the purpose and audience they are writing for.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child will write stories about real or imagined events using good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain complex ideas clearly by choosing and organizing information well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child will write arguments that support their claims with good reasons and enough evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child uses clues from the text to figure out or fix words they don’t know, rereading when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level prose and poetry orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings
description_plain: Your child reads stories and poems out loud with accuracy, good speed, and expression.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child reads grade-level text with a clear purpose and understands what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child reads accurately and smoothly enough to understand the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read grade-appropriate irregularly spelled words.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-appropriate words that don’t follow usual spelling rules.

subject: Language Arts
description: Decode words with common Latin suffixes.
description_plain: Your child can read words that have common Latin endings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and know the meaning of the most common prefixes and derivational suffixes.
description_plain: Your child knows common beginnings and endings of words and what they mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child uses phonics and word parts to read new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend informational texts, including history/social studies, science, and technical texts, at the high end of the grades 2—3 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: By the end of third grade, your child can read and understand harder nonfiction texts on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the most important points and key details presented in two texts on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child can find and explain similarities and differences in important points from two texts on the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the logical connection between particular sentences and paragraphs in a text (e.g., comparison, cause/effect, first/second/third in a sequence).
description_plain: Your child can explain how sentences and paragraphs connect, like showing cause and effect or order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use information gained from illustrations (e.g., maps, photographs) and the words in a text to demonstrate understanding of the text (e.g., where, when, why, and how key events occur).
description_plain: Your child uses pictures and words in a text to understand key details like where and when events happen.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish their own point of view from that of the author of a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between their own opinion and the author’s point of view.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use text features and search tools (e.g., key words, sidebars, hyperlinks) to locate information relevant to a given topic efficiently.
description_plain: Your child uses text features like headings and keywords to quickly find information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases in a text relevant to a grade 3 topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of important words related to the topic they are reading about.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the relationship between a series of historical events, scientific ideas or concepts, or steps in technical procedures in a text, using language that pertains to time, sequence, and cause/effect.
description_plain: Your child explains how events or ideas in a text are connected by time or cause and effect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main idea of a text; recount the key details and explain how they support the main idea.
description_plain: Your child finds the main idea of a text and explains how key details support it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to demonstrate understanding of a text, referring explicitly to the text as the basis for the answers.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions about a text using details from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poetry, at the high end of the grades 2—3 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: By the end of third grade, your child can read and understand harder stories, plays, and poems on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the themes, settings, and plots of stories written by the same author about the same or similar characters (e.g., in books from a series).
description_plain: Your child compares the themes, settings, and plots of stories by the same author about similar characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how specific aspects of a text's illustrations contribute to what is conveyed by the words in a story (e.g., create mood, emphasize aspects of a character or setting).
description_plain: Your child explains how pictures in a story help show feelings, settings, or important parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish their own point of view from that of the narrator or those of the characters.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between their own view and the narrator’s or characters’ views.

subject: Language Arts
description: Refer to parts of stories, dramas, and poems when writing or speaking about a text, using terms such as chapter, scene, and stanza; describe how each successive part builds on earlier sections.
description_plain: Your child talks or writes about parts of stories, plays, or poems using words like chapter or stanza, and explains how parts build on each other.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, distinguishing literal from nonliteral language.
description_plain: Your child figures out what words and phrases mean in a story, including when they are used in a literal or figurative way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe characters in a story (e.g., their traits, motivations, or feelings) and explain how their actions contribute to the sequence of events.
description_plain: Your child describes characters’ traits and feelings and explains how their actions move the story forward.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recount stories, including fables, folktales, and myths from diverse cultures; determine the central message, lesson, or moral and explain how it is conveyed through key details in the text.
description_plain: Your child retells stories from different cultures and explains the main message or lesson using details from the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions to demonstrate understanding of a text, referring explicitly to the text as the basis for the answers.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions about a story using details from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and information on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts talk about the same ideas or topics and explain the different ways the authors write about them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and think about the main points and reasons in a text and decide if the evidence is strong and relevant.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and judge information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the author's point of view or purpose affects what is written and how it is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can look at how sentences and paragraphs fit together in a text and explain how they help the whole story or article.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases in a text, including special or figurative meanings, and explain how word choices affect the feeling or meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas change and connect throughout a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize the important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what a text says and make logical guesses, using examples from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Social Studies
description: Use digital tools to learn about social studies concepts.
description_plain: Your child can use computers and other digital tools to learn about social studies topics.

subject: Social Studies
description: Use digital tools to learn about social studies concepts.
description_plain: Your child can use computers and other digital tools to learn about social studies topics.

subject: Social Studies
description: Use digital tools to learn about social studies concepts.

description: Distinguish between fiction and non-fiction.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between made-up stories and real facts.

subject: Social Studies
description: Distinguish between fiction and non-fiction.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between made-up stories and real facts.

subject: Social Studies
description: Distinguish between fiction and non-fiction.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between made-up stories and real facts.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify what kinds of information can be found in different resources (e.g., library, computer, atlas, and dictionary).
description_plain: Your child can recognize what kind of information is found in places like libraries, computers, atlases, and dictionaries.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify what kinds of information can be found in different resources (e.g., library, computer, atlas, and dictionary).
description_plain: Your child can recognize what kind of information is found in places like libraries, computers, atlases, and dictionaries.

subject: Social Studies
description: Technology, Literacy, and Global Connections: Students use technology and literacy skills to access, synthesize, and evaluate information to communicate and apply social studies knowledge to global situations.
description_plain: Your child can use technology and reading skills to find, understand, and share information about social studies and world events.

description_plain: Your child can use technology and reading skills to find, understand, and share information about social studies and world events.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify how people, including Indigenous Tribes of Wyoming, may adjust to and/or change their environment in order to survive (e.g., clothing, houses, foods, and natural resources).
description_plain: Your child understands how people, including Wyoming’s Indigenous Tribes, change or adapt their clothing, homes, food, and use of natural resources to live.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify how people, including Indigenous Tribes of Wyoming, may adjust to and/or change their environment in order to survive (e.g., clothing, houses, foods, and natural resources).
description_plain: Your child understands how people, including Wyoming’s Indigenous Tribes, change or adapt their clothing, homes, food, and use of natural resources to live.

subject: Social Studies
description: Use the human features of a community to describe what makes that community unique (e.g., cultural, language, religion, food, clothing, political, economic, population, and types of jobs in an area) and why others move to or from that place.
description_plain: Your child can describe what makes a community special by looking at things like culture, language, food, jobs, and why people move there or leave.

subject: Social Studies
description: Use the human features of a community to describe what makes that community unique (e.g., cultural, language, religion, food, clothing, political, economic, population, and types of jobs in an area) and why others move to or from that place.
description_plain: Your child can describe what makes a community special by looking at things like culture, language, food, jobs, and why people move there or leave.

subject: Social Studies

description_plain: Your child can describe what makes a community special by looking at things like culture, language, food, jobs, and why people move there or leave.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify, describe, and use local physical and human characteristics to discuss the similarities and differences between parts of the community (e.g., neighborhoods, schools, towns, and reservation communities).
description_plain: Your child can talk about how different parts of the community are alike or different by looking at places like neighborhoods, schools, and towns.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify, describe, and use local physical and human characteristics to discuss the similarities and differences between parts of the community (e.g., neighborhoods, schools, towns, and reservation communities).
description_plain: Your child can talk about how different parts of the community are alike or different by looking at places like neighborhoods, schools, and towns.

subject: Social Studies
description: Use a map, globe, and mental mapping to identify familiar areas and simple patterns and create maps using various media.
description_plain: Your child can use maps, globes, and their own ideas to find familiar places and make simple maps.

subject: Social Studies

subject: Social Studies
description: People, Places, and Environments: Students apply their knowledge of the geographic themes (location, place, movement, region, and human/environment interactions) and skills to demonstrate an understanding of interrelationships among people, places, and environment.
description_plain: Your child can use what they know about places, people, and the environment to understand how they all connect.

subject: Social Studies
description: Describe a "current event" involving significant people and places in Wyoming (e.g., local, state, or tribal events).
description_plain: Your child can talk about a recent important event involving people and places in Wyoming.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify tools and technologies, including those of Indigenous Tribes of Wyoming, that made or make life easier and sustainable (e.g., cars for getting one place to another, washing machines for washing clothes, flashlights to see in the dark, and usage of bison and natural resources).
description_plain: Your child can identify tools and technology, including those used by Wyoming’s Indigenous Tribes, that help people live easier and better lives.

description_plain: Your child can identify tools and technology, including those used by Wyoming’s Indigenous Tribes, that help people live easier and better lives.

subject: Social Studies
description: Identify how an event could change the future (e.g., moving to a new town means going to a new school or learning to ride a bike could mean getting to a friend's house faster).
description_plain: Your child understands how an event, like moving or learning a new skill, can change what happens next.

subject: Social Studies
description: Time, Continuity, and Change: Students analyze events, people, problems, and ideas within their historical contexts.
description_plain: Your child can look at events, people, and ideas from history and understand them in their own time.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases; gather vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child will learn and use new academic and subject-specific words correctly to help understand and express ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among the connotations (associations) of words with similar denotations (definitions) (e.g., refined, respectful, polite, diplomatic, condescending).
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between words that have similar meanings but different feelings or associations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words (e.g., synonym/antonym, analogy) to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child uses relationships between words, like synonyms and antonyms, to better understand their meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g., literary, biblical, and mythological allusions) in context.
description_plain: Your child can explain the meaning of figures of speech, like references to stories or myths, based on the context.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase (e.g., by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary).
description_plain: Your child checks the meaning of a word or phrase by looking at how it’s used or by using a dictionary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult general and specialized reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning or its part of speech.
description_plain: Your child uses dictionaries, glossaries, or thesauruses, in print or online, to find how to say a word, what it means, or its part of speech.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek or Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., belligerent, bellicose, rebel).
description_plain: Your child uses common Greek or Latin word parts to help figure out the meaning of new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence or paragraph; a word's position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child uses clues from the sentence or paragraph to understand the meaning of a word or phrase.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 7 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of unknown or multiple-meaning words using different strategies based on what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose language that expresses ideas precisely and concisely, recognizing and eliminating wordiness and redundancy.
description_plain: Your child chooses words that clearly and briefly express ideas, avoiding extra or repeated words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses correct language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a comma to separate coordinate adjectives (e.g., It was a fascinating, enjoyable movie but not He wore an old[,] green shirt).
description_plain: Your child uses commas correctly between adjectives that equally describe a noun, like in 'a fascinating, enjoyable movie.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Place phrases and clauses within a sentence, recognizing and correcting misplaced and dangling modifiers.
description_plain: Your child places phrases and clauses correctly in sentences and fixes misplaced or unclear modifiers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Choose among simple, compound, complex, and compound-complex sentences to signal differing relationships among ideas.
description_plain: Your child chooses different sentence types to show how ideas are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain the function of phrases and clauses in general and their function in specific sentences.
description_plain: Your child explains what phrases and clauses do in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses standard grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses a wide range of academic and subject-specific words for reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and can find out new word meanings on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of unknown or tricky words by using clues from the sentence, breaking the word into parts, or looking them up in dictionaries or other resources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about language to understand how it works in different situations and to choose words and styles that fit what they want to say or understand better when reading or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and word usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child changes how they speak depending on the situation and uses formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include multimedia components and visual displays in presentations to clarify claims and findings and emphasize salient points.
description_plain: Your child adds pictures, videos, or other media to presentations to help explain ideas and highlight important points.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and findings, emphasizing salient points in a focused, coherent manner with pertinent descriptions, facts, details, and examples; use appropriate eye contact, adequate volume, and clear pronunciation.
description_plain: Your child presents ideas clearly and focuses on important points using facts, details, and examples, while speaking with good eye contact, volume, and pronunciation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate a speaker's argument and specific claims, evaluating the soundness of the reasoning and the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child explains a speaker’s argument and checks if the reasons and evidence make sense and support the claims.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the main ideas and supporting details presented in diverse media and formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively, orally) and explain how the ideas clarify a topic, text, or issue under study.
