description_plain: Your child uses what they know about language to understand how it works in different situations and to choose words and styles that fit what they want to say or understand better when reading or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and word usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Differentiate between contexts that call for formal English (e.g., presenting ideas) and situations where informal discourse is appropriate (e.g., small-group discussion); use formal English when appropriate to task and situation.
description_plain: Your child knows when to use formal English, like during presentations, and when informal talk is okay, like in small group chats.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add audio recordings and visual displays to presentations when appropriate to enhance the development of main ideas or themes.
description_plain: Your child adds audio or pictures to presentations to help explain main ideas better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Report on a topic or text, tell a story, or recount an experience in an organized manner, using appropriate facts and relevant, descriptive details to support main ideas or themes; speak clearly at an understandable pace.
description_plain: Your child can tell about a topic, story, or experience in an organized way using facts and details, speaking clearly and at a good speed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the reasons and evidence a speaker provides to support particular points.
description_plain: Your child can find the reasons and evidence a speaker uses to support their points.

subject: Language Arts
description: Paraphrase portions of a text read aloud or information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child can restate parts of a story or information they hear or see in their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Review the key ideas expressed and explain their own ideas and understanding in light of the discussion.
description_plain: Your child can explain the main ideas from a discussion and share their own thoughts about it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose and respond to specific questions to clarify or follow up on information, and make comments that contribute to the discussion and link to the remarks of others.
description_plain: Your child asks and answers questions to understand better and adds comments that connect to what others say during discussions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions and carry out assigned roles.
description_plain: Your child follows the rules for group talks and does their assigned part.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation and other information known about the topic to explore ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared by reading or studying and uses what they learned to share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 4 topics and texts, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child takes part in different kinds of group talks, listens to others, and shares their own ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child changes how they speak depending on the situation and uses formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child uses digital tools and visuals to share information and make presentations easier to understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child shares information and evidence clearly so listeners can follow their ideas and the way they organize their talk fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child thinks about a speaker’s opinion, reasons, and evidence to decide how strong their argument is.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child combines and evaluates information from different sources like pictures, numbers, and spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child gets ready for and takes part in conversations with different people, building on others’ ideas and sharing their own clearly and confidently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over short and long periods for different subjects, reasons, and audiences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 4 Reading standards to informational texts (e.g., "Explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support particular points in a text").
description_plain: Your child uses reading skills to explain how authors support points with reasons and evidence in informational texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 4 Reading standards to literature (e.g., "Describe in depth a character, setting, or event in a story or drama, drawing on specific details in the text [e.g., a character's thoughts, words, or actions].").
description_plain: Your child uses reading skills to describe characters, settings, or events in stories by using details from the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child finds evidence from stories or informational texts to support their thinking, reflection, and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recall relevant information from experiences or gather relevant information from print and digital sources; take notes and categorize information, and provide a list of sources.
description_plain: Your child remembers or finds information from books or online, takes notes, organizes it, and lists where they got it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects that build knowledge through investigation of different aspects of a topic.
description_plain: Your child does short research projects to learn about different parts of a topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from adults, use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing as well as to interact and collaborate with others; demonstrate sufficient command of keyboarding skills to type a minimum of one page in a single sitting.
description_plain: With some help, your child uses technology like the Internet to write, publish, and work with others, and can type at least one page in one sitting.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, and editing.
description_plain: With help from others, your child plans, revises, and edits their writing to make it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development and organization are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child writes clearly and organizes their ideas to fit the task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that follows from the experiences or events they described.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use concrete words and phrases and sensory details to convey experiences and events precisely.
description_plain: Your child uses clear words and sensory details to describe experiences and events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transitional words and phrases to manage the sequence of events.
description_plain: Your child uses different transition words to show the order of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use dialogue and description to develop experiences and events or show the responses of characters to situations.
description_plain: Your child uses dialogue and descriptions to show experiences and how characters react.

subject: Language Arts
description: Orient the reader by establishing a situationand introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally.
description_plain: Your child starts stories by setting the scene and introducing characters, then tells events in a natural order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, descriptive details, and clear event sequences.
description_plain: Your child writes stories about real or imagined events using good techniques, details, and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the information or explanation presented
description_plain: Your child writes a closing statement or section that relates to the information or explanation they gave.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use clear and specific words related to the topic to explain or share information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link ideas within categories of information using words and phrases (e.g., another, for example, also, because).
description_plain: Your child will connect ideas using words like 'another,' 'for example,' and 'because' to make writing flow.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples related to the topic.
description_plain: Your child can add facts, definitions, details, or examples to explain a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly and group related information in paragraphs and sections; include formatting (e.g., headings), illustrations, and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child will start writing by clearly introducing the topic and organizing information into paragraphs, using headings or pictures when helpful.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas and information clearly.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain a topic and share ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section related to the opinion presented.
description_plain: Your child will write a closing sentence or section that relates to their opinion.

subject: Language Arts
description: Link opinion and reasons using words and phrases (e.g., for instance, in order to, in addition).
description_plain: Your child can connect their opinion and reasons using words like 'for instance' or 'in addition.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide reasons that are supported by facts and details.
description_plain: Your child will give reasons for their opinion that are supported by facts and details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic or text clearly, state an opinion, and create an organizational structure in which related ideas are grouped to support the writer's purpose.
description_plain: Your child can clearly introduce a topic, state their opinion, and organize ideas to support their point.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write opinion pieces on topics or texts, supporting a point of view with reasons and information
description_plain: Your child will write opinion pieces that share their point of view with reasons and information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly over short and long periods for different tasks and audiences, including time to research and revise.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child will find and use evidence from stories or information texts to support their thinking and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from books and online sources, check if the information is trustworthy, and use it without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child will do short and longer research projects by asking questions and showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology like the Internet to write, publish, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child will improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to express ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organized for the purpose and audience they are writing for.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child will write stories about real or imagined events using good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain complex ideas clearly by choosing and organizing information well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child will write arguments that support their claims with good reasons and enough evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context to confirm or self-correct word recognition and understanding, rereading as necessary.
description_plain: Your child can use clues from the sentence to figure out or fix words they don’t know, rereading if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level prose and poetry orally with accuracy, appropriate rate, and expression on successive readings
description_plain: Your child will read stories and poems out loud with accuracy, good speed, and expression.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read on-level text with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read grade-level text with a clear purpose and understanding.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read with sufficient accuracy and fluency to support comprehension.
description_plain: Your child will read accurately and smoothly enough to understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use combined knowledge of all letter-sound correspondences, syllabication patterns, and morphology (e.g., roots and affixes) to read accurately unfamiliar multisyllabic words in context and out of context.
description_plain: Your child can use knowledge of letter sounds, syllables, and word parts to read new longer words correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child will use grade-level phonics and word skills to sound out and understand words.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of year, read and comprehend informational texts, including history/social studies, science, and technical texts, in the grades 4—5 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: By the end of fourth grade, your child can read and understand informational texts about history, science, and technical subjects with some help for harder parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate information from two texts on the same topic in order to write or speak about the subject knowledgeably.
description_plain: Your child can combine information from two texts on the same topic to talk or write about it knowledgeably.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support particular points in a text.
description_plain: Your child will explain how an author uses reasons and evidence to support points in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret information presented visually, orally, or quantitatively (e.g., in charts, graphs, diagrams, time lines, animations, or interactive elements on Web pages) and explain how the information contributes to an understanding of the text in which it appears.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain information shown in pictures, charts, or graphs and how it helps explain the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast a firsthand and secondhand account of the same event or topic; describe the differences in focus and the information provided.
description_plain: Your child will compare a firsthand and secondhand account of the same event and describe how they are different.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe the overall structure (e.g., chronology, comparison, cause/effect, problem/solution) of events, ideas, concepts, or information in a text or part of a text.
description_plain: Your child can describe how a text is organized, like by time order, cause and effect, or problem and solution.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of general academic and domain-specific words or phrases in a text relevant to a grade 4 topic or subject area.
description_plain: Your child will figure out the meaning of important words related to grade 4 topics in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain events, procedures, ideas, or concepts in a historical, scientific, or technical text, including what happened and why, based on specific information in the text.
description_plain: Your child can explain events or ideas in history, science, or technical texts, including what happened and why.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the main idea of a text and explain how it is supported by key details; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child will find the main idea of a text, explain how details support it, and summarize the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Refer to details and examples in a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can use details and examples from a text to explain what it says and to make inferences.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poetry, in the grades 4—5 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: By the end of fourth grade, your child can read and understand stories, plays, and poems at their grade level, with some help for harder parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the treatment of similar themes and topics (e.g., opposition of good and evil) and patterns of events (e.g., the quest) in stories, myths, and traditional literature from different cultures.
description_plain: Your child will compare how similar themes and story patterns appear in stories and myths from different cultures.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make connections between the text of a story or drama and a visual or oral presentation of the text, identifying where each version reflects specific descriptions and directions in the text.
description_plain: Your child can connect a story or play to its visual or oral presentation by identifying where the presentation matches the text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the point of view from which different stories are narrated, including the difference between first- and third-person narrations.
description_plain: Your child will compare different points of view in stories, including the difference between first-person and third-person narrations.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain major differences between poems, drama, and prose, and refer to the structural elements of poems (e.g., verse, rhythm, meter) and drama (e.g., casts of characters, settings, descriptions, dialogue, stage directions) when writing or speaking about a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the main differences between poems, plays, and stories, and talk about parts of poems like rhythm and parts of plays like characters and dialogue.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including those that allude to significant characters found in mythology (e.g., Herculean).
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a story, including words that refer to famous characters from myths.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe in depth a character, setting, or event in a story or drama, drawing on specific details in the text (e.g., a character's thoughts, words, or actions).
description_plain: Your child can describe a character, place, or event in a story using details like what a character thinks, says, or does.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme of a story, drama, or poem from details in the text; summarize the text.
description_plain: Your child can find the main message or theme of a story, play, or poem and tell what the text is about in a short summary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Refer to details and examples in a text when explaining what the text says explicitly and when drawing inferences from the text.
description_plain: Your child can use examples from a story to explain what it says clearly and to make guesses about what it means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and information on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts talk about the same ideas or topics and explain the different ways the authors write about them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and think about the main points and reasons in a text and decide if the evidence is strong and relevant.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and judge information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the author's point of view or purpose affects what is written and how it is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can look at how sentences and paragraphs fit together in a text and explain how they help the whole story or article.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases in a text, including special or figurative meanings, and explain how word choices affect the feeling or meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas change and connect throughout a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize the important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what a text says and make logical guesses, using examples from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words and phrases acquired through conversations, reading and being read to, and responding to texts.
description_plain: Your child can use new words and phrases learned from talking, reading, and listening to stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish shades of meaning among verbs describing the same general action (e.g., walk, march, strut, prance) by acting out the meanings.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between similar action words (like walk, march, strut) by acting them out.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify real-life connections between words and their use (e.g., note places at school that are colorful).
description_plain: Your child can connect words to real-life places or things, like noticing colorful spots at school.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of frequently occurring verbs and adjectives by relating them to their opposites (antonyms).
description_plain: Your child can understand common verbs and adjectives by knowing their opposites.

subject: Language Arts
description: Sort common objects into categories (e.g., shapes, foods) to gain a sense of the concepts the categories represent.
description_plain: Your child can group common objects, like shapes or foods, to understand what the groups mean.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, explore word relationships and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: With help from adults, your child explores how words are related and the different meanings they can have.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the most frequently occurring inflections and affixes (e.g., -ed, -s, re-, un-, pre-, -ful, -less) as a clue to the meaning of an unknown word.
description_plain: Your child can use common word parts like -ed or re- to help figure out the meaning of new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify new meanings for familiar words and apply them accurately (e.g., knowing duck is a bird and learning the verb to duck).
description_plain: Your child can learn new meanings for familiar words and use them correctly, like knowing 'duck' as a bird and also as a verb.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on kindergarten reading and content.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words based on what they read and learn in kindergarten.

subject: Language Arts
description: Spell simple words phonetically, drawing on knowledge of sound-letter relationships.
description_plain: Your child can spell simple words by sounding them out using what they know about letters and sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write a letter or letters for most consonant and short-vowel sounds (phonemes).
description_plain: Your child can write letters for most consonant and short vowel sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and name end punctuation.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and name punctuation marks at the end of sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Capitalize the first word in a sentence and the pronoun I.
description_plain: Your child can capitalize the first word in a sentence and the pronoun I.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce and expand complete sentences in shared language activities.
description_plain: Your child can make and add to complete sentences during shared speaking activities.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the most frequently occurring prepositions (e.g., to, from, in, out, on, off, for, of, by, with).
description_plain: Your child can use common prepositions like to, from, in, and on.

subject: Language Arts
description: Understand and use question words (interrogatives) (e.g., who, what, where, when, why, how).
description_plain: Your child can understand and use question words like who, what, where, when, why, and how.

subject: Language Arts
description: Form regular plural nouns orally by adding /s/ or /es/ (e.g., dog, dogs; wish, wishes).
description_plain: Your child can say plural nouns by adding -s or -es, like dogs or wishes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use frequently occurring nouns and verbs.
description_plain: Your child can use common nouns and verbs.

subject: Language Arts
description: Print many upper- and lowercase letters.
description_plain: Your child can print many uppercase and lowercase letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and word use when speaking or writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when encountering an unknown term important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses a wide range of academic and subject-specific words for reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and can find out new word meanings on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of unknown or tricky words by using clues from the sentence, breaking the word into parts, or looking them up in dictionaries or other resources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about language to understand how it works in different situations and to choose words and styles that fit what they want to say or understand better when reading or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and word usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Speak audibly and express thoughts, feelings, and ideas clearly.
description_plain: Your child can speak clearly so others can understand their thoughts and feelings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add drawings or other visual displays to descriptions as desired to provide additional detail.
description_plain: Your child can add drawings or pictures to their descriptions to give more details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe familiar people, places, things, and events and, with prompting and support, provide additional detail.
description_plain: Your child can describe familiar people, places, things, and events and add more details with help.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions in order to seek help, get information, or clarify something that is not understood.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer questions to get help, learn new information, or understand better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Confirm understanding of a text read aloud or information presented orally or through other media by asking and answering questions about key details and requesting clarification if something is not understood.
description_plain: Your child can show they understand a story or information by asking and answering questions and asking for help if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Continue a conversation through multiple exchanges.
description_plain: Your child can keep a conversation going by taking turns talking several times.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow agreed-upon rules for discussions (e.g., listening to others and taking turns speaking about the topics and texts under discussion).
description_plain: Your child can follow simple rules during conversations, like listening carefully and taking turns to speak about topics or stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in collaborative conversations with diverse partners about kindergarten topics and texts with peers and adults in small and larger groups.
description_plain: Your child will join in group talks with classmates and adults about kindergarten topics and stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child changes how they speak depending on the situation and uses formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child uses digital tools and visuals to share information and make presentations easier to understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child shares information and evidence clearly so listeners can follow their ideas and the way they organize their talk fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child thinks about a speaker’s opinion, reasons, and evidence to decide how strong their argument is.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child combines and evaluates information from different sources like pictures, numbers, and spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child gets ready for and takes part in conversations with different people, building on others’ ideas and sharing their own clearly and confidently.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, recall information from experiences or gather information from provided sources to answer a question.
description_plain: With help, your child can remember information from experiences or given sources to answer questions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Participate in shared research and writing projects (e.g., explore a number of books by a favorite author and express opinions about them).
description_plain: Your child will take part in group research and writing projects, like reading books by a favorite author and sharing opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, explore a variety of digital tools to produce and publish writing, including in collaboration with peers.
description_plain: With support, your child can use different digital tools to create and share writing, sometimes working with friends.

subject: Language Arts
description: With guidance and support from adults, respond to questions and suggestions from peers and add details to strengthen writing as needed.
description_plain: With help, your child can listen to friends' ideas about their writing and add details to make it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a combination of drawing, dictating, and writing to narrate a single event or several loosely linked events, tell about the events in the order in which they occurred, and provide a reaction to what happened.
description_plain: Your child can tell a story about one or more events by drawing, speaking, or writing, sharing what happened in order and how they felt.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a combination of drawing, dictating, and writing to compose informative/explanatory texts in which they name what they are writing about and supply some information about the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use drawing, speaking, or writing to explain a topic by naming it and giving some information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a combination of drawing, dictating, and writing to compose opinion pieces in which they tell a reader the topic or the name of the book they are writing about and state an opinion or preference about the topic or book (e.g., My favorite book is…).
description_plain: Your child can share their opinion about a topic or book by drawing, speaking, or writing, telling what they like or think.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly over short and long periods for different tasks and audiences, including time to research and revise.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child will find and use evidence from stories or information texts to support their thinking and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from books and online sources, check if the information is trustworthy, and use it without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child will do short and longer research projects by asking questions and showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology like the Internet to write, publish, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child will improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to express ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organized for the purpose and audience they are writing for.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child will write stories about real or imagined events using good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain complex ideas clearly by choosing and organizing information well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child will write arguments that support their claims with good reasons and enough evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read emergent-reader texts with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can read simple books carefully and understand what they read.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish between similarly spelled words by identifying the sounds of the letters that differ.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between words that look alike by noticing the different letter sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read common high-frequency words by sight (e.g., the, of, to, you, she, my, is, are, do, does).
description_plain: Your child can recognize and read common sight words like 'the,' 'you,' and 'is' quickly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Associate the long and short sounds with common spellings (graphemes) for the five major vowels.
description_plain: Your child can connect the long and short vowel sounds to the letters that usually make those sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate basic knowledge of one-to-one letter-sound correspondences by producing the primary or many of the most frequent sound for each consonant.
description_plain: Your child can say the main sounds that letters make, especially consonants.

subject: Language Arts
description: Know and apply grade-level phonics and word analysis skills in decoding words.
description_plain: Your child can use letter sounds and word parts to sound out and read words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Add or substitute individual sounds (phonemes) in simple, one-syllable words to make new words.
description_plain: Your child can change or add sounds in simple one-syllable words to make new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Isolate and pronounce the initial, medial vowel, and final sounds (phonemes) in three-phoneme (consonent-vowel-consonent, or CVC) words. (This does not include CVCs ending with /l/, /r/, or /x/.)
description_plain: Your child can say the first, middle vowel, and last sounds in simple three-letter words like 'cat' or 'dog.'

subject: Language Arts
description: Blend and segment onsets and rimes of single-syllable spoken words.
description_plain: Your child can break apart and put together the beginning and ending parts of one-syllable spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Count, pronounce, blend, and segment syllables in spoken words.
description_plain: Your child can count, say, blend, and break apart the beats (syllables) in spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and produce rhyming words.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and say words that rhyme.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of spoken words, syllables, and sounds (phonemes).
description_plain: Your child understands and works with spoken words, syllables, and sounds.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and name all upper- and lowercase letters of the alphabet.
description_plain: Your child can recognize and name all uppercase and lowercase letters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Understand that words are separated by spaces in print.
description_plain: Your child knows that words in print are separated by spaces.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize that spoken words are represented in written language by specific sequences of letters.
description_plain: Your child understands that spoken words are shown by specific letters in writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow words from left to right, top to bottom, and page by page.
description_plain: Your child can follow words in a book from left to right, top to bottom, and page by page.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of the organization and basic features of print.
description_plain: Your child understands how print is organized and its basic features.

subject: Language Arts
description: Actively engage in group reading activities with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can join group reading activities and understand what is read.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify basic similarities in and differences between two texts on the same topic (e.g., in illustrations, descriptions, or procedures).
description_plain: With help, your child can find similarities and differences between two texts about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify the reasons an author gives to support points in a text.
description_plain: With support, your child can identify reasons an author gives to explain points in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, describe the relationship between illustrations and the text in which they appear (e.g., what person, place, thing, or idea in the text an illustration depicts).
description_plain: With help, your child can explain how pictures relate to the text, like what they show from the story or information.

subject: Language Arts
description: Name the author and illustrator of a text and define the role of each in presenting the ideas or information in a text.
description_plain: Your child can name the author and illustrator of a book and understand what each person does.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify the front cover, back cover, and title page of a book.
description_plain: Your child can identify the front cover, back cover, and title page of a book.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about unknown words in a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can ask and answer questions about words they don’t know in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, describe the connection between two individuals, events, ideas, or pieces of information in a text.
description_plain: With support, your child can describe how two people, events, ideas, or facts in a text are connected.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify the main topic and retell key details of a text.
description_plain: With help, your child can tell the main topic of a text and share important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: With support, your child can ask and answer questions about important details in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Actively engage in group reading activities with purpose and understanding.
description_plain: Your child can take part in group reading activities and understand the stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, compare and contrast the adventures and experiences of characters in familiar stories.
description_plain: With help, your child can compare the adventures and experiences of characters in familiar stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, describe the relationship between illustrations and the story in which they appear (e.g., what moment in a story an illustration depicts).
description_plain: With support, your child can explain how pictures in a story show parts of the story, like a special moment.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, name the author and illustrator of a story and define the role of each in telling the story.
description_plain: Your child can name the author and illustrator of a story and understand what each person does to tell the story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize common types of texts (e.g., storybooks, poems).
description_plain: Your child can recognize different kinds of books, like storybooks and poems.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ask and answer questions about unknown words in a text.
description_plain: Your child can ask about and answer questions when they find words they don’t know in a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, identify characters, settings, and major events in a story.
description_plain: With some help, your child can point out the characters, where the story happens, and the main events.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, retell familiar stories, including key details.
description_plain: With some help, your child can retell familiar stories and include important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: With prompting and support, ask and answer questions about key details in a text.
description_plain: With some help, your child can ask and answer questions about important parts of a story.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and information on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts talk about the same ideas or topics and explain the different ways the authors write about them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and think about the main points and reasons in a text and decide if the evidence is strong and relevant.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and judge information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the author's point of view or purpose affects what is written and how it is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can look at how sentences and paragraphs fit together in a text and explain how they help the whole story or article.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases in a text, including special or figurative meanings, and explain how word choices affect the feeling or meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas change and connect throughout a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize the important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what a text says and make logical guesses, using examples from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately grade-appropriate general academic and domain-specific words and phrases; gather vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use new academic and subject-specific words correctly to help understand and express ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among the connotations (associations) of words with similar denotations (definitions) (e.g., stingy, scrimping, economical, unwasteful, thrifty).
description_plain: Your child can tell the different feelings or ideas that similar words can have, like how 'stingy' and 'thrifty' are different.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use the relationship between particular words (e.g., cause/effect, part/whole, item/category) to better understand each of the words.
description_plain: Your child can use the relationship between words, like cause and effect or part and whole, to understand their meanings better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g., personification) in context.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain figures of speech, like when something is given human qualities.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase (e.g., by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary).
description_plain: Your child can check the meaning of a word or phrase by looking at how it is used or by using a dictionary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning or its part of speech.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries, glossaries, or thesauruses, in print or online, to find how to say a word, what it means, or its part of speech.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use common, grade-appropriate Greek or Latin affixes and roots as clues to the meaning of a word (e.g., audience, auditory, audible).
description_plain: Your child can use common Greek or Latin roots and prefixes to help figure out the meaning of new words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use context (e.g., the overall meaning of a sentence or paragraph; a word's position or function in a sentence) as a clue to the meaning of a word or phrase.
description_plain: Your child can use the words around an unknown word and its place in a sentence to help understand what it means.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases based on grade 6 reading and content, choosing flexibly from a range of strategies.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of new or tricky words using different strategies like context clues or reference tools.

subject: Language Arts
description: Maintain consistency in style and tone.
description_plain: Your child can keep the same style and tone when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Vary sentence patterns for meaning, reader/listener interest, and style.
description_plain: Your child can change sentence patterns to make writing or speaking more interesting and clear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use knowledge of language and its conventions when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about language rules when writing, speaking, reading, or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use punctuation (commas, parentheses, dashes) to set off nonrestrictive/parenthetical elements.
description_plain: Your child can use commas, parentheses, and dashes correctly to add extra information in sentences.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child can use correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize variations from standard English in their own and others' writing and speaking, and identify and use strategies to improve expression in conventional language.
description_plain: Your child can notice when language is different from standard English and use ways to improve their writing and speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct vague pronouns (i.e., ones with unclear or ambiguous antecedents).
description_plain: Your child can find and fix unclear pronouns that don’t clearly show who or what they refer to.

subject: Language Arts
description: Recognize and correct inappropriate shifts in pronoun number and person.
description_plain: Your child can spot and correct mistakes when pronouns change in number or person inappropriately.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use intensive pronouns (e.g., myself, ourselves).
description_plain: Your child can use intensive pronouns like 'myself' and 'ourselves' correctly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Ensure that pronouns are in the proper case (subjective, objective, possessive).
description_plain: Your child can use pronouns in the correct form depending on their role in a sentence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child can use correct grammar and language rules when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately a range of general academic and domain-specific words and phrases sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child learns and uses a wide range of academic and subject-specific words for reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and can find out new word meanings on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands figurative language, how words relate to each other, and subtle differences in word meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine or clarify the meaning of unknown and multiple-meaning words and phrases by using context clues, analyzing meaningful word parts, and consulting general and specialized reference materials, as appropriate.
description_plain: Your child figures out the meaning of unknown or tricky words by using clues from the sentence, breaking the word into parts, or looking them up in dictionaries or other resources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply knowledge of language to understand how language functions in different contexts, to make effective choices for meaning or style, and to comprehend more fully when reading or listening.
description_plain: Your child uses what they know about language to understand how it works in different situations and to choose words and styles that fit what they want to say or understand better when reading or listening.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.
description_plain: Your child uses correct capitalization, punctuation, and spelling when writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate command of the conventions of standard English grammar and usage when writing or speaking.
description_plain: Your child uses correct grammar and word usage when writing or speaking.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child can change how they speak to fit different situations and use formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Include multimedia components (e.g., graphics, images, music, sound) and visual displays in presentations to clarify information.
description_plain: Your child can add pictures, music, or other media to presentations to help explain ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present claims and findings, sequencing ideas logically and using pertinent descriptions, facts, and details to accentuate main ideas or themes; use appropriate eye contact, adequate volume, and clear pronunciation.
description_plain: Your child can share ideas or findings in a clear order using facts and details, while making good eye contact and speaking clearly and loudly enough.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate a speaker's argument and specific claims, distinguishing claims that are supported by reasons and evidence from claims that are not.
description_plain: Your child can explain a speaker’s argument and tell which claims have good reasons and evidence and which do not.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret information presented in diverse media and formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively, orally) and explain how it contributes to a topic, text, or issue under study.
description_plain: Your child can understand information shown in different ways, like pictures, numbers, or spoken words, and explain how it helps explain a topic or issue.

subject: Language Arts
description: Review the key ideas expressed and demonstrate understanding of multiple perspectives through reflection and paraphrasing.
description_plain: Your child can think about and explain the main ideas and different points of view by putting them into their own words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Pose and respond to specific questions with elaboration and detail by making comments that contribute to the topic, text, or issue under discussion.
description_plain: Your child can ask and answer detailed questions that add to the discussion about a topic or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow rules for collegial discussions, set specific goals and deadlines, and define individual roles as needed.
description_plain: Your child can follow rules for group talks, set clear goals and deadlines, and understand their role in the group.

subject: Language Arts
description: Come to discussions prepared, having read or studied required material; explicitly draw on that preparation by referring to evidence on the topic, text, or issue to probe and reflect on ideas under discussion.
description_plain: Your child comes to discussions prepared by reading or studying and uses what they learned to ask questions and share ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage effectively in a range of collaborative discussions (one-on-one, in groups, and teacher-led) with diverse partners on grade 6 topics, texts, and issues, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly.
description_plain: Your child can take part in different kinds of group talks, listen to others, and share their own ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Adapt speech to a variety of contexts and communicative tasks, demonstrating command of formal English when indicated or appropriate.
description_plain: Your child changes how they speak depending on the situation and uses formal English when needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Make strategic use of digital media and visual displays of data to express information and enhance understanding of presentations.
description_plain: Your child uses digital tools and visuals to share information and make presentations easier to understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Present information, findings, and supporting evidence such that listeners can follow the line of reasoning and the organization, development, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child shares information and evidence clearly so listeners can follow their ideas and the way they organize their talk fits the purpose and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Evaluate a speaker's point of view, reasoning, and use of evidence and rhetoric.
description_plain: Your child thinks about a speaker’s opinion, reasons, and evidence to decide how strong their argument is.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate information presented in diverse media and formats, including visually, quantitatively, and orally.
description_plain: Your child combines and evaluates information from different sources like pictures, numbers, and spoken words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Prepare for and participate effectively in a range of conversations and collaborations with diverse partners, building on others' ideas and expressing their own clearly and persuasively.
description_plain: Your child gets ready for and takes part in conversations with different people, building on others’ ideas and sharing their own clearly and confidently.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for reflection and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over short and long periods for different subjects, purposes, and readers, taking time to think and improve their work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child uses facts and information from nonfiction texts to support their thinking and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, using search terms effectively; assess the credibility and accuracy of each source; and quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and following a standard format for citation.
description_plain: Your child finds useful information from books and the internet, checks if the sources are trustworthy, and shares the information correctly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question (including a self-generated question), drawing on several sources and generating additional related, focused questions that allow for multiple avenues of exploration.
description_plain: Your child does short research projects to answer questions, using several sources and asking new related questions to learn more.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and present the relationships between information and ideas clearly and efficiently.
description_plain: Your child uses technology like the internet to write and share their ideas clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach, focusing on how well purpose and audience have been addressed.
description_plain: With some help, your child improves their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to better reach their readers.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child writes clearly and in an organized way that fits the task, purpose, and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from and supports the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that supports the information or explanation they shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style and objective tone.
description_plain: Your child writes in a formal and objective way.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child uses clear and specific words related to the topic to explain or inform.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate and varied transitions to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child uses different transition words to connect ideas and make their writing easy to follow.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant, well-chosen facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child adds facts, definitions, details, and examples to explain the topic well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic clearly, previewing what is to follow; organize ideas, concepts, and information into broader categories as appropriate to achieving purpose; include formatting (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., charts, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child introduces a topic clearly, organizes ideas into groups, and uses headings, charts, or pictures to help readers understand.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts, including the narration of historical events, scientific procedures/experiments, or technical processes.
description_plain: Your child writes informative texts that explain events, scientific steps, or technical processes.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from or supports the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that supports the argument they made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child writes in a formal style.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and clauses to create cohesion and clarify the relationships among claim(s), counterclaims, reasons, and evidence.
description_plain: Your child uses words and phrases to connect claims, counterclaims, reasons, and evidence clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with logical reasoning and relevant, accurate data and evidence that demonstrate an understanding of the topic or text, using credible sources.
description_plain: Your child supports their claims with clear reasons and accurate facts from trustworthy sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s) about a topic or issue, acknowledge and distinguish the claim(s) from alternate or opposing claims, and organize the reasons and evidence logically.
description_plain: Your child introduces their claim about a topic, recognizes other views, and organizes reasons and evidence logically.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments focused on discipline-specific content.
description_plain: Your child writes arguments focused on specific subjects.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of discipline-specific tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child writes regularly over short and long times for different subjects, purposes, and readers, including time to research and revise.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 6 Reading standards to literary nonfiction (e.g., "Trace and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, distinguishing claims that are supported by reasons and evidence from claims that are not").
description_plain: Your child looks closely at nonfiction texts to understand and evaluate the arguments and claims.

subject: Language Arts
description: Apply grade 6 Reading standards to literature (e.g., "Compare and contrast texts in different forms or genres [e.g., stories and poems; historical novels and fantasy stories] in terms of their approaches to similar themes and topics").
description_plain: Your child compares and contrasts different types of stories or poems to understand similar themes or topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child uses evidence from stories or informational texts to support their thinking and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources; assess the credibility of each source; and quote or paraphrase the data and conclusions of others while avoiding plagiarism and providing basic bibliographic information for sources.
description_plain: Your child finds information from books and online, checks if it is trustworthy, and shares it correctly without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short research projects to answer a question, drawing on several sources and refocusing the inquiry when appropriate.
description_plain: Your child does short research projects to answer questions, using several sources and changing focus if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing as well as to interact and collaborate with others; demonstrate sufficient command of keyboarding skills to type a minimum of three pages in a single sitting.
description_plain: Your child uses technology to write, publish, and work with others, and can type at least three pages in one sitting.

subject: Language Arts
description: With some guidance and support from peers and adults, develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: With some help, your child improves their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child writes clearly and organizes their ideas to fit the task and audience.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a conclusion that follows from the narrated experiences or events.
description_plain: Your child writes a conclusion that follows from the story or events they shared.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise words and phrases, relevant descriptive details, and sensory language to convey experiences and events.
description_plain: Your child uses exact words, details, and sensory language to describe experiences and events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use a variety of transition words, phrases, and clauses to convey sequence and signal shifts from one time frame or setting to another.
description_plain: Your child uses different transition words to show the order of events and changes in time or place.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use narrative techniques, such as dialogue, pacing, and description, to develop experiences, events, and/or characters.
description_plain: Your child uses storytelling techniques like dialogue and description to develop events and characters.

subject: Language Arts
description: Engage and orient the reader by establishing a context and introducing a narrator and/or characters; organize an event sequence that unfolds naturally and logically.
