description_plain: Your child starts a story by setting the scene and introducing characters, and organizes events in a clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, relevant descriptive details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child can write stories about real or made-up events using clear details and a good order of events.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from the information or explanation presented.
description_plain: Your child will write a closing sentence or section that sums up the information or explanation.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child understands how to keep a formal tone in their writing.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use precise language and domain-specific vocabulary to inform about or explain the topic.
description_plain: Your child can use exact words and special vocabulary to explain a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use appropriate transitions to clarify the relationships among ideas and concepts.
description_plain: Your child uses linking words to show how ideas and concepts connect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop the topic with relevant facts, definitions, concrete details, quotations, or other information and examples.
description_plain: Your child adds facts, definitions, details, quotes, or examples to explain the topic well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce a topic; organize ideas, concepts, and information, using strategies such as definition, classification, comparison/contrast, and cause/effect; include formatting (e.g., headings), graphics (e.g., charts, tables), and multimedia when useful to aiding comprehension.
description_plain: Your child introduces a topic and organizes ideas clearly, using tools like headings, charts, or pictures to help explain.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine a topic and convey ideas, concepts, and information through the selection, organization, and analysis of relevant content
description_plain: Your child writes informative texts that explain a topic by choosing and organizing important facts and ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Provide a concluding statement or section that follows from the argument presented.
description_plain: Your child writes a closing statement that follows the argument they made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Establish and maintain a formal style.
description_plain: Your child keeps a formal style when writing arguments.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use words, phrases, and clauses to clarify the relationships among claim(s) and reasons.
description_plain: Your child uses words and phrases to clearly show how their claims and reasons connect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Support claim(s) with clear reasons and relevant evidence, using credible sources and demonstrating an understanding of the topic or text.
description_plain: Your child supports their claims with clear reasons and evidence from trustworthy sources.

subject: Language Arts
description: Introduce claim(s) and organize the reasons and evidence clearly.
description_plain: Your child introduces their claims and organizes reasons and evidence clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims with clear reasons and relevant evidence.
description_plain: Your child writes arguments that support their claims with clear reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write routinely over extended time frames (time for research, reflection, and revision) and shorter time frames (a single sitting or a day or two) for a range of tasks, purposes, and audiences.
description_plain: Your child can write regularly over short and long periods for different tasks and audiences, including time to research and revise.

subject: Language Arts
description: Draw evidence from literary or informational texts to support analysis, reflection, and research.
description_plain: Your child will find and use evidence from stories or information texts to support their thinking and research.

subject: Language Arts
description: Gather relevant information from multiple print and digital sources, assess the credibility and accuracy of each source, and integrate the information while avoiding plagiarism.
description_plain: Your child can gather information from books and online sources, check if the information is trustworthy, and use it without copying.

subject: Language Arts
description: Conduct short as well as more sustained research projects based on focused questions, demonstrating understanding of the subject under investigation.
description_plain: Your child will do short and longer research projects by asking questions and showing they understand the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Use technology, including the Internet, to produce and publish writing and to interact and collaborate with others.
description_plain: Your child can use technology like the Internet to write, publish, and work with others.

subject: Language Arts
description: Develop and strengthen writing as needed by planning, revising, editing, rewriting, or trying a new approach.
description_plain: Your child will improve their writing by planning, revising, editing, or trying new ways to express ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Produce clear and coherent writing in which the development, organization, and style are appropriate to task, purpose, and audience.
description_plain: Your child can write clearly and organized for the purpose and audience they are writing for.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write narratives to develop real or imagined experiences or events using effective technique, well-chosen details, and well-structured event sequences.
description_plain: Your child will write stories about real or imagined events using good details and clear order.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write informative/explanatory texts to examine and convey complex ideas and information clearly and accurately through the effective selection, organization, and analysis of content.
description_plain: Your child can write informative texts that explain complex ideas clearly by choosing and organizing information well.

subject: Language Arts
description: Write arguments to support claims in an analysis of substantive topics or texts, using valid reasoning and relevant and sufficient evidence.
description_plain: Your child will write arguments that support their claims with good reasons and enough evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of grade 8, read and comprehend science/technical texts in the grades 6—8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand science and technical texts for grades 6-8 on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the information gained from experiments, simulations, video, or multimedia sources with that gained from reading a text on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child compares information from experiments, videos, or simulations with what they read about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among facts, reasoned judgment based on research findings, and speculation in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between facts, opinions based on research, and guesses in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate quantitative or technical information expressed in words in a text with a version of that information expressed visually (e.g., in a flowchart, diagram, model, graph, or table).
description_plain: Your child combines information written in words with pictures like charts or graphs to understand it better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the author's purpose in providing an explanation, describing a procedure, or discussing an experiment in a text.
description_plain: Your child figures out why the author explains something, describes a process, or talks about an experiment.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure an author uses to organize a text, including how the major sections contribute to the whole and to an understanding of the topic.
description_plain: Your child looks at how the author organizes the text and how each part helps explain the topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of symbols, key terms, and other domain-specific words and phrases as they are used in a specific scientific or technical context relevant to grades 6—8 texts and topics.
description_plain: Your child understands the meaning of special words and symbols used in science or technical texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Follow precisely a multistep procedure when carrying out experiments, taking measurements, or performing technical tasks.
description_plain: Your child follows detailed steps carefully when doing experiments or technical tasks.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the central ideas or conclusions of a text; provide an accurate summary of the text distinct from prior knowledge or opinions.
description_plain: Your child finds the main ideas or conclusions in a text and summarizes them clearly without adding personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of science and technical texts.
description_plain: Your child uses specific examples from the text to support their understanding of science and technical topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of grade 8, read and comprehend history/social studies texts in the grades 6—8 text complexity band independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand history and social studies texts for grades 6-8 on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the relationship between a primary and secondary source on the same topic.
description_plain: Your child compares how a primary source and a secondary source present information about the same topic.

subject: Language Arts
description: Distinguish among fact, opinion, and reasoned judgment in a text.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between facts, opinions, and thoughtful judgments in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate visual information (e.g., in charts, graphs, photographs, videos, or maps) with other information in print and digital texts.
description_plain: Your child uses pictures, charts, or maps along with written information to understand a topic better.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify aspects of a text that reveal an author's point of view or purpose (e.g., loaded language, inclusion or avoidance of particular facts).
description_plain: Your child notices parts of a text that show the author's opinion or purpose, like strong words or missing facts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a text presents information (e.g., sequentially, comparatively, causally).
description_plain: Your child explains how a text organizes information, like in order, by comparison, or by cause and effect.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including vocabulary specific to domains related to history/social studies.
description_plain: Your child understands the meaning of words and phrases used in history and social studies texts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify key steps in a text's description of a process related to history/social studies (e.g., how a bill becomes law, how interest rates are raised or lowered).
description_plain: Your child identifies important steps in a process described in history or social studies, like how a law is made.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the central ideas or information of a primary or secondary source; provide an accurate summary of the source distinct from prior knowledge or opinions.
description_plain: Your child finds the main ideas in a primary or secondary source and summarizes them clearly without personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite specific textual evidence to support analysis of primary and secondary sources.
description_plain: Your child uses specific examples from history or social studies texts to support their understanding.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literary nonfiction in the grades 6—8 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand nonfiction books and articles for grades 6-8, with some help for harder parts.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast one author's presentation of events with that of another (e.g., a memoir written by and a biography on the same person).
description_plain: Your child compares how two authors tell about the same events, like in a memoir and a biography.

subject: Language Arts
description: Trace and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, distinguishing claims that are supported by reasons and evidence from claims that are not.
description_plain: Your child follows and evaluates arguments in a text, telling which claims have good reasons and evidence.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate information presented in different media or formats (e.g., visually, quantitatively) as well as in words to develop a coherent understanding of a topic or issue.
description_plain: Your child combines information from pictures, numbers, and words to understand a topic clearly.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine an author's point of view or purpose in a text and explain how it is conveyed in the text.
description_plain: Your child understands the author's point of view or purpose and explains how the author shows it.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a particular sentence, paragraph, chapter, or section fits into the overall structure of a text and contributes to the development of the ideas.
description_plain: Your child explains how a sentence, paragraph, or section fits into the whole text and helps develop the ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative, connotative, and technical meanings.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including when they have special or technical meanings.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze in detail how a key individual, event, or idea is introduced, illustrated, and elaborated in a text (e.g., through examples or anecdotes).
description_plain: Your child can explain how an important person, event, or idea is introduced and described in a text using examples or stories.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a central idea of a text and how it is conveyed through particular details; provide a summary of the text distinct from personal opinions or judgments.
description_plain: Your child can find the main idea of a text and explain it using details, then summarize the text without adding personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find and use parts of the text to support what it says clearly and to explain ideas they figure out from reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: By the end of the year, read and comprehend literature, including stories, dramas, and poems, in the grades 6—8 text complexity band proficiently, with scaffolding as needed at the high end of the range.
description_plain: By the end of the year, your child will read and understand stories, plays, and poems that are suitable for grades 6 to 8, with some help if needed.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast texts in different forms or genres (e.g., stories and poems; historical novels and fantasy stories) in terms of their approaches to similar themes and topics.
description_plain: Your child can compare different kinds of texts, like stories and poems, and explain how they handle similar themes or topics.

subject: Language Arts
description: Compare and contrast the experience of reading a story, drama, or poem to listening to or viewing an audio, video, or live version of the text, including contrasting what they "see" and "hear" when reading the text to what they perceive when they listen or watch.
description_plain: Your child can compare reading a story, play, or poem to listening to or watching it, noticing differences in what they see and hear.

subject: Language Arts
description: Explain how an author develops the point of view of the narrator or speaker in a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the author shows the narrator's or speaker's point of view in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how a particular sentence, chapter, scene, or stanza fits into the overall structure of a text and contributes to the development of the theme, setting, or plot.
description_plain: Your child can explain how a sentence, chapter, scene, or stanza fits into the whole text and helps develop the theme, setting, or plot.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine the meaning of words and phrases as they are used in a text, including figurative and connotative meanings; analyze the impact of a specific word choice on meaning and tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out what words and phrases mean in a text, including special meanings, and explain how word choices affect the meaning and feeling.

subject: Language Arts
description: Describe how a particular story's or drama's plot unfolds in a series of episodes as well as how the characters respond or change as the plot moves toward a resolution.
description_plain: Your child can describe how a story or play's plot happens in steps and how the characters change as the story moves toward the ending.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine a theme or central idea of a text and how it is conveyed through particular details; provide a summary of the text distinct from personal opinions or judgments.
description_plain: Your child can find the theme or main idea of a text and explain it using details, then summarize the text without personal opinions.

subject: Language Arts
description: Cite textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly as well as inferences drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can find parts of the text to support what it says clearly and to explain ideas they understand from reading.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read and comprehend complex literary and informational texts independently and proficiently.
description_plain: Your child can read and understand difficult stories and information on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how two or more texts address similar themes or topics in order to build knowledge or to compare the approaches the authors take.
description_plain: Your child can compare how two or more texts talk about the same ideas or topics and explain the different ways the authors write about them.

subject: Language Arts
description: Delineate and evaluate the argument and specific claims in a text, including the validity of the reasoning as well as the relevance and sufficiency of the evidence.
description_plain: Your child can find and think about the main points and reasons in a text and decide if the evidence is strong and relevant.

subject: Language Arts
description: Integrate and evaluate content presented in diverse media and formats, including visually and quantitatively, as well as in words.
description_plain: Your child can understand and judge information presented in different ways, like pictures, numbers, and words.

subject: Language Arts
description: Assess how point of view or purpose shapes the content and style of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how the author's point of view or purpose affects what is written and how it is written.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze the structure of texts, including how specific sentences, paragraphs, and larger portions of the text (e.g., a section, chapter, scene, or stanza) relate to each other and the whole.
description_plain: Your child can look at how sentences and paragraphs fit together in a text and explain how they help the whole story or article.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret words and phrases as they are used in a text, including determining technical, connotative, and figurative meanings, and analyze how specific word choices shape meaning or tone.
description_plain: Your child can figure out the meaning of words and phrases in a text, including special or figurative meanings, and explain how word choices affect the feeling or meaning.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze how and why individuals, events, and ideas develop and interact over the course of a text.
description_plain: Your child can explain how people, events, and ideas change and connect throughout a story or text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Determine central ideas or themes of a text and analyze their development; summarize the key supporting details and ideas.
description_plain: Your child can find the main ideas or themes in a text, explain how they develop, and summarize the important details.

subject: Language Arts
description: Read closely to determine what the text says explicitly and to make logical inferences from it; cite specific textual evidence when writing or speaking to support conclusions drawn from the text.
description_plain: Your child can read carefully to understand what a text says and make logical guesses, using examples from the text to support their ideas.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use academic and subject-specific words correctly when reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and can find out new word meanings on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Acquire and use accurately general academic and domain-specific words and phrases, sufficient for reading, writing, speaking, and listening at the college and career readiness level; demonstrate independence in gathering vocabulary knowledge when considering a word or phrase important to comprehension or expression.
description_plain: Your child can learn and use academic and subject-specific words correctly when reading, writing, speaking, and listening, and can find out new word meanings on their own.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze nuances in the meaning of words with similar denotations.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain small differences in the meanings of words that are similar.

subject: Language Arts
description: Analyze nuances in the meaning of words with similar denotations.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain small differences in the meanings of words that are similar.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g., hyperbole, paradox) in context and analyze their role in the text.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain how figures of speech like exaggerations or contradictions work in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Interpret figures of speech (e.g., hyperbole, paradox) in context and analyze their role in the text.
description_plain: Your child can understand and explain how figures of speech like exaggerations or contradictions work in a text.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands how figurative language, word relationships, and subtle differences in word meanings work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Demonstrate understanding of figurative language, word relationships, and nuances in word meanings.
description_plain: Your child understands how figurative language, word relationships, and subtle differences in word meanings work.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase (e.g., by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary).
description_plain: Your child can check if their first guess about a word’s meaning is correct by looking at the context or using a dictionary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Verify the preliminary determination of the meaning of a word or phrase (e.g., by checking the inferred meaning in context or in a dictionary).
description_plain: Your child can check if their first guess about a word’s meaning is correct by looking at the context or using a dictionary.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult general and specialized reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning, its part of speech, its etymology, or its standard usage.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries, glossaries, and thesauruses, both printed and online, to find how to say a word, what it means, its part of speech, origin, or proper use.

subject: Language Arts
description: Consult general and specialized reference materials (e.g., dictionaries, glossaries, thesauruses), both print and digital, to find the pronunciation of a word or determine or clarify its precise meaning, its part of speech, its etymology, or its standard usage.
description_plain: Your child can use dictionaries, glossaries, and thesauruses, both printed and online, to find how to say a word, what it means, its part of speech, origin, or proper use.

subject: Language Arts
description: Identify and correctly use patterns of word changes that indicate different meanings or parts of speech (e.g., conceive, conception, conceivable).

subject: Math
description: Represent the concept of multiplication of whole numbers using models and strategies with partial success.

subject: Math

subject: Math
description: Represent and solve problems involving multiplication and division.

subject: Math

subject: Math

subject: Math
description: Use appropriate tools strategically.

subject: Math
description: Construct viable arguments and critique the reasoning of others.

subject: Math
description: Reason abstractly and quantitatively.

subject: Math
description: Make sense of problems and persevere in solving them.

subject: Math

description: Regarding angles, recognize angles as geometric shapes that are formed wherever two rays share a common endpoint.
description_plain: Your child recognizes that angles are shapes formed when two rays meet at a point.

subject: Math
description: Geometric measurement: understand concepts of angle and measure angles.

subject: Math
description:
The Below Basic student may be able to:
  • Make a line plot to display a data set of measurements in fractions of a unit (½, ¼, 1/8) with partial success. OR
  • Solve problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions by using information presented in given line plots with partial success.
description_plain: Your child may be able to make a line plot with fractional measurements or solve simple fraction addition and subtraction problems using line plots, but may need help.

subject: Math
description:
The Basic student is able to make a line plot to:
  • Display a data set of measurements in fractions of a unit (½, ¼, 1/8). OR
  • Solve problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions by using information presented in given line plots.
description_plain: Your child can make a line plot showing measurements with fractions like ½, ¼, or 1/8, and solve addition and subtraction problems using the line plots.

subject: Math
description: Make a line plot to display a data set of measurements in fractions of a unit (½, ¼, 1/8). Solve problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions by using information presented in line plots.
description_plain: Your child can make a line plot with fractional measurements and solve addition and subtraction problems using the information from the line plots.

subject: Math
description:
The Advanced student is able to
  • Solve problems involving multiplication of fractions by using information presented in line plots. AND
  • Use multiplicative thinking to generalize data from a line plot.
description_plain: Your child can solve multiplication problems with fractions using line plots and use multiplication to understand data from line plots.

subject: Math
description: Make a line plot to display a data set of measurements in fractions of a unit (½, ¼, 1/8). Solve problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions by using information presented in line plots.
description_plain: Your child can make a line plot with fractional measurements and solve addition and subtraction problems using the line plots.

subject: Math
description: May be able to determine the area and perimeter of rectangles with partial success when given a labeled (length and width) pictorial representation.
description_plain: Your child may be able to find the area and perimeter of rectangles using pictures with length and width labeled, but may need help.

subject: Math
description: Determine the area and perimeter of rectangles when given a labeled (length and width) pictorial representation.

subject: Math
description: Apply the area and perimeter formulas for rectangles in real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can use formulas to find the area and perimeter of rectangles in real-life and math problems.

subject: Math
description:
The Advanced student is able to:
  • Apply the formula for area of a rectangle to find the area of a right triangle. OR
  • Find the largest possible area of a rectangle when given a specific perimeter. OR
  • Find the largest possible perimeter of a rectangle when given a specific area.
description_plain: Your child can use the area formula for rectangles to find the area of right triangles, or figure out the biggest area or perimeter a rectangle can have when given certain limits.

subject: Math
description: Apply the area and perimeter formulas for rectangles in real-world and mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can use formulas to find the area and perimeter of rectangles in everyday and math problems.

subject: Math
description: The Below Basic student may be able to use the four operations to solve one-step word problems (involving distances, intervals of time, liquid volumes, masses of objects, and money) which require expressing measurements given in a larger unit in terms of a smaller unit with partial success, when given diagrams that feature a measurement scale.
description_plain: Your child may be able to solve simple word problems about distances, time, volume, weight, and money using addition, subtraction, multiplication, or division, especially when given pictures with measurement scales.

subject: Math
description: The Basic student is able to use the four operations to solve one-step word problems (involving distances, intervals of time, liquid volumes, masses of objects, and money) which require expressing measurements given in a larger unit in terms of a smaller unit when given diagrams that feature a measurement scale.
description_plain: Your child can solve one-step word problems about distances, time, volume, weight, and money using the four math operations and convert measurements from bigger units to smaller units when given pictures with measurement scales.

subject: Math
description: The Proficient student is able to use the four operations to solve word problems involving distances, intervals of time, liquid volumes, masses of objects, and money, including problems involving simple fractions or decimals, and problems that require expressing measurements given in a larger unit in terms of a smaller unit. Represent measurement quantities using diagrams such as number line diagrams that feature a measurement scale.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems using addition, subtraction, multiplication, or division about distances, time, volume, weight, and money, including simple fractions or decimals, and change measurements from bigger units to smaller units using number line diagrams.

subject: Math
description: In addition to Proficient, the Advanced student is able to create and solve real-world scenarios using the four operations involving distances, intervals of time, liquid volumes, masses of objects, and money, including problems involving simple fractions or decimals, and problems that require expressing measurements given in a larger unit in terms of a smaller unit or smaller units in terms of larger units. Represent measurement quantities using diagrams such as number line diagrams that feature a measurement scale.
description_plain: Your child can create and solve real-life problems using the four math operations about distances, time, volume, weight, and money, including fractions or decimals, and convert measurements both ways between bigger and smaller units using number line diagrams.

subject: Math
description: Use the four operations to solve word problems involving distances, intervals of time, liquid volumes, masses of objects, and money, including problems involving simple fractions or decimals, and problems that require expressing measurements given in a larger unit in terms of a smaller unit. description_plain: Your child can solve word problems using addition, subtraction, multiplication, or division about distances, time, volume, weight, and money, including simple fractions or decimals, and change measurements from bigger units to smaller units using diagrams like number lines.

subject: Math
description: May be able to, within a single system of measurement, express measurements in a larger unit in terms of a smaller unit with partial success when given a labeled model or two-column table.

subject: Math
description: Within a single system of measurement, express measurements in a larger unit in terms of a smaller unit when given a labeled model or two-column table.

subject: Math
description: Know relative sizes of measurement units within one system of units including, but not limited to, km, m, cm; kg, g; lb, oz.; l L, ml; hr, min, sec; ft., in., gal., qt. pt., c. subject: Math

subject: Math
description: Know relative sizes of measurement units within one system of units including, but not limited to, km, m, cm; kg, g; lb., oz.; l L, ml; hr., min, sec; ft., in., gal., qt. pt., c. description_plain: Your child understands how different units of measurement compare within one system and can change measurements from bigger units to smaller units and write these changes in a two-column table.

subject: Math

  • Explain how to use the digits in multi-digit whole numbers to round numbers up to 1,000,000. OR

  • Justify the appropriate place value to which the student would round in a given situation.
description_plain: Your child can explain how to round large numbers, show why rounding helps with math, or decide the best place to round in different situations.

subject: Math

subject: Math

description_plain: Your child can explain and use how digits in different places relate by tens, compare very large numbers, and understand place value beyond just one place.

description_plain: Your child can understand and solve problems where a fraction is divided by a whole number, using pictures to show the work.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems involving division of unit fractions by non-zero whole numbers and division of whole numbers by unit fractions by using visual fraction models and equations to represent the problem.

subject: Math
description: Interpret division of a whole number by a unit fraction and compute the quotient.

subject: Math
description: Interpret division of a unit fraction by a non-zero whole number and compute the quotient.

subject: Math
description: The Proficient student is able to extend the concept of division to divide unit fractions and whole numbers by using visual fraction models and equations.
description_plain: Your child understands how to divide fractions and whole numbers using pictures and equations.

subject: Math
description:
The Advanced student is able to:
  • Create and solve a real-world problem and justify how the model/equation relates to the problem. OR
  • Solve real-world problems involving division of unit fractions by non-zero whole numbers and division of whole numbers using multiple representations.
description_plain: Your child can create and solve real-world problems about dividing fractions and whole numbers, and explain how their pictures or equations match the problem.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems involving division of unit fractions by non-zero whole numbers and division of whole numbers by unit fractions by using visual fraction models and equations to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life problems involving dividing fractions by whole numbers and whole numbers by fractions, using pictures and equations.

subject: Math
description: Interpret division of a whole number by a unit fraction and compute the quotient.
description_plain: Your child can understand and solve division problems where a whole number is divided by a fraction.

subject: Math
description: Interpret division of a unit fraction by a non-zero whole number and compute the quotient.
description_plain: Your child can understand and solve division problems where a fraction is divided by a whole number.

subject: Math
description: Extend the concept of division to divide unit fractions and whole numbers by using visual fraction models and equations.
description_plain: Your child learns to divide fractions and whole numbers using pictures and equations.

subject: Math
description: May be able to solve real-world problems involving multiplication of fractions and whole numbers, with partial success, when given visual fraction models or equations to represent the problem.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems involving multiplication of fractions and whole numbers when given visual fraction models or equations to represent the problem.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems involving multiplication of fractions and mixed numbers by using visual fraction models or equations to represent the problem.

subject: Math
description:
The Advanced student is able to:
  • Solve multi-step real-world problems involving multiplication of fractions including mixed numbers with multiple strategies or representations. OR
  • Create and solve real-world problems involving multiplication of fractions and mixed numbers by creating visual fraction models or equations to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve multi-step real-life problems multiplying fractions and mixed numbers in different ways, or create and solve their own problems using pictures or equations.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems involving multiplication of fractions and mixed numbers by using visual fraction models or equations to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life problems multiplying fractions and mixed numbers using pictures or equations.

subject: Math
description: Estimate the size of the product based on the size of the two factors with partial success.

subject: Math
description: The Basic student is able to estimate the size of the product based on the size of the two factors.

subject: Math

subject: Math
description: Explain why multiplying a given number by a fraction less than 1 results in a product smaller than the given number.

description: Interpret multiplication of a fraction by a whole number and a whole number by a fraction and compute the product.

subject: Math

subject: Math

description_plain: Your child can draw pictures to show how to multiply fractions or find a missing side of a rectangle when given the area and one side.

subject: Math
description: Interpret multiplication in which both factors are fractions less than one and compute the product.
description_plain: Your child understands how to multiply two fractions less than one and can find the answer.

description_plain: Your child can multiply a fraction by a whole number and a whole number by a fraction and find the product.

subject: Math
description: May be able to interpret a fraction as division of the numerator by the denominator (a/b = a ÷ b) with partial success, when given a visual model. Solve word problems involving division of whole numbers leading to answers in the form of fractions or mixed numbers, with partial success, when given visual fraction models or equations to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child may begin to understand that a fraction means dividing the top number by the bottom number and can try solving word problems with help from pictures or equations.

subject: Math
description: Interpret a fraction as division of the numerator by the denominator (a/b = a ÷ b) when given a visual model. Solve word problems involving division of whole numbers leading to answers in the form of fractions or mixed numbers when given visual fraction models or equations to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child understands that a fraction means dividing the numerator by the denominator and can solve word problems using pictures or equations.

subject: Math
description: Interpret a fraction as division of the numerator by the denominator (a/b = a ÷ b). Solve word problems involving division of whole numbers leading to answers in the form of fractions or mixed numbers by using visual fraction models or equations to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child understands that a fraction is the result of dividing the numerator by the denominator and can solve word problems using pictures or equations.

subject: Math
description: Create a real-world situation and visual model to demonstrate understanding between division of whole numbers and fractions.
description_plain: Your child can create a real-life example and draw a picture to show how dividing whole numbers relates to fractions.

subject: Math
description: Interpret a fraction as division of the numerator by the denominator (a/b = a ÷ b). Solve word problems involving division of whole numbers leading to answers in the form of fractions or mixed numbers by using visual fraction models or equations to represent the problem.
description_plain: Your child understands that a fraction means dividing the numerator by the denominator and can solve word problems using pictures or equations.

subject: Math
description: Apply and extend previous understandings of multiplication and division to multiply and divide fractions.
description_plain: Your child will use what they know about multiplication and division to work with fractions.

subject: Math
description: May be able to solve one-step mathematical problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions referring to the same whole, including cases of unlike denominators with partial success, when given visual fraction models or equations to represent the problem. Use benchmark fractions and number sense of fractions to estimate mentally and assess the reasonableness of answers.
description_plain: Your child may begin to solve simple addition and subtraction problems with fractions that have different bottom numbers, using pictures or equations, and can estimate answers using common fractions.

subject: Math
description: Solve one-step mathematical problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions referring to the same whole, including cases of unlike denominators, when given visual fraction models or equations to represent the problem. Use benchmark fractions and number sense of fractions to estimate mentally and assess the reasonableness of answers.
description_plain: Your child can solve addition and subtraction problems with fractions that have different bottom numbers using pictures or equations and can estimate answers using common fractions.

subject: Math
description: Solve word problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions referring to the same whole, including cases of unlike denominators, e.g., by using visual fraction models or equations to represent the problem. Use benchmark fractions and number sense of fractions to estimate mentally and assess the reasonableness of answers.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems adding and subtracting fractions with different bottom numbers using pictures or equations and can estimate answers using common fractions.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world problems involving addition or subtraction with at least 3 or more fractions with unlike denominators (including mixed numbers) by replacing given fractions with equivalent fractions in such a way as to produce an equivalent sum or difference of fractions with like denominators. Use benchmark fractions and number sense of fractions to estimate mentally and assess the reasonableness of answers.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life problems adding or subtracting three or more fractions with different bottom numbers by changing them to equivalent fractions with the same bottom number and can estimate answers using common fractions.

subject: Math
description: Solve word problems involving addition and subtraction of fractions referring to the same whole, including cases of unlike denominators, e.g., by using visual fraction models or equations to represent the problem. Use benchmark fractions and number sense of fractions to estimate mentally and assess the reasonableness of answers.
description_plain: Your child can solve word problems adding and subtracting fractions with different bottom numbers using pictures or equations and can estimate answers using common fractions.

subject: Math
description: May be able to add and subtract fractions with unlike denominators by replacing given fractions with equivalent fractions in such a way as to produce an equivalent sum or difference of fractions with like denominators, with partial success, when given a visual model.
description_plain: Your child may begin to add and subtract fractions with different bottom numbers by changing them to equivalent fractions with the same bottom number, using pictures.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract fractions with unlike denominators by replacing given fractions with equivalent fractions in such a way as to produce an equivalent sum or difference of fractions with like denominators when given a visual model.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract fractions with different bottom numbers by changing them into equal fractions with the same bottom number using pictures.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract fractions with unlike denominators (including mixed numbers) by replacing given fractions with equivalent fractions in such a way as to produce an equivalent sum or difference of fractions with like denominators.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract fractions and mixed numbers with different bottom numbers by changing them into equal fractions with the same bottom number.

subject: Math
description: Add or subtract three or more fractions with unlike denominators (including mixed numbers) that require regrouping by replacing the given fractions with equivalent fractions and justify the denominators chosen.
description_plain: Your child can add or subtract three or more fractions and mixed numbers with different bottom numbers by changing them into equal fractions and explain why they chose those bottom numbers.

subject: Math
description: Add and subtract fractions with unlike denominators (including mixed numbers) by replacing given fractions with equivalent fractions in such a way as to produce an equivalent sum or difference of fractions with like denominators.
description_plain: Your child can add and subtract fractions and mixed numbers with different bottom numbers by changing them into equal fractions with the same bottom number.

subject: Math
description: Use equivalent fractions as a strategy to add and subtract fractions.
description_plain: Your child understands how to use equal fractions to help add and subtract fractions.

subject: Math

description: Find volumes of right rectangular prisms with whole-number edge lengths in the context of solving real-world and mathematical problems given the formulas V = (l)(w)(h) and V = (B)(h) for rectangular prisms with partial success.
description_plain: Your child can find the volume of rectangular boxes with whole-number sides using formulas, sometimes needing help.

subject: Math
description: Find the volume of a right rectangular prism with whole number dimensions by multiplying them with partial success. OR
description_plain: Your child can multiply whole numbers to find the volume of rectangular boxes, sometimes needing help.

subject: Math
description:
The Basic student is able to:
  • Find the volume of a right rectangular prism with whole number dimensions by multiplying them. Show that this volume is the same as when counting unit cubes. OR
  • Find volumes of right rectangular prisms with whole-number edge lengths in the context of solving real-world and mathematical problems given the formulas V = (l)(w)(h) and V = (B)(h) for rectangular prisms.
description_plain: Your child can find the volume of rectangular boxes by multiplying side lengths and show this matches counting cubes, or use formulas to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Find volumes of right rectangular prisms with whole-number edge lengths in the context of solving real-world and mathematical problems given the formulas V = (l)(w)(h) and V = (B)(h) for rectangular prisms.
description_plain: Your child can find the volume of rectangular boxes with whole-number sides using formulas to solve real-life and math problems.

subject: Math
description: Find the volume of a right rectangular prism with whole number dimensions by multiplying them. Show that this volume is the same as when counting unit cubes.
description_plain: Your child can multiply whole numbers to find the volume of rectangular boxes and show this matches counting cubes.

subject: Math
description: Relate volume to the operations of multiplication and solve real-world and mathematical problems involving volume.
description_plain: Your child understands how volume relates to multiplication and can solve problems about volume.

subject: Math
description:
The Advanced student is able to:
  • Solve real-world problems by finding volumes of solid figures composed of two non-overlapping right rectangular prisms by adding the volumes of the non-overlapping parts. OR
  • Find the base or height when given volume and some of the dimensions. OR
  • Find multiple possible dimensions of a right rectangular prism with a given volume.
description_plain: Your child can solve volume problems with shapes made of two boxes, find missing sides when given volume, or find different side lengths for a given volume.

subject: Math
description: Find volumes of right rectangular prisms with whole-number edge lengths in the context of solving real-world and mathematical problems given the formulas V = (l)(w)(h) and V = (B)(h) for rectangular prisms.
description_plain: Your child can find the volume of rectangular boxes with whole-number sides using formulas to solve problems.

subject: Math
description: Find the volume of a right rectangular prism with whole number dimensions by multiplying them. Show that this volume is the same as when counting unit cubes.
description_plain: Your child can multiply whole numbers to find the volume of rectangular boxes and show this matches counting cubes.

subject: Math
description: Relate volume to the operations of multiplication and solve real-world and mathematical problems involving volume.
description_plain: Your child understands how volume connects to multiplication and can solve volume problems.

subject: Math
description: May be able to measure volumes by counting unit cubes, using cubic cm, cubic in, cubic ft, and improvised units, with partial success, when given a partially completed visual model.
description_plain: Your child can sometimes measure volume by counting cubes or using units like cubic inches, with some help and a picture to guide them.

subject: Math
description: Measure volumes by counting unit cubes, using cubic cm, cubic in, cubic ft, and improvised units when given a partially completed visual model.
description_plain: Your child can measure volume by counting cubes or using units like cubic centimeters or cubic feet with a picture to help.

subject: Math
description: Measure volumes by counting unit cubes, using cubic cm, cubic in, cubic ft., and improvised units.
description_plain: Your child can measure volume by counting cubes or using units like cubic centimeters, inches, or feet.

subject: Math
description:
The Advanced student is able to:
  • Use an efficient counting strategy (not counting one unit at a time) and determine appropriate measurement units. OR
  • Explain how you would find volume in a compound figure.
description_plain: Your child can use smart ways to count volume without counting one cube at a time and can explain how to find volume in shapes made of more than one box.

subject: Math
description: Measure volumes by counting unit cubes, using cubic cm, cubic in, cubic ft., and improvised units.
description_plain: Your child can measure volume by counting cubes or using units like cubic centimeters, inches, or feet.

subject: Math
description: The Below Basic student may be able to identify the use of volume and the appropriate measurement for a given situation or model with partial success.
description_plain: Your child can sometimes tell when to use volume and which measurement to use, with some help.

subject: Math
description: The Basic student is able to identify the use of volume and the appropriate measurement for a given situation or model.
description_plain: Your child can tell when to use volume and pick the right measurement for a situation or model.

subject: Math
description: The Proficient student is able to recognize volume as an attribute of three-dimensional figures and understand concepts of volume measurement such as "unit cube" and a volume of n cubic units.
description_plain: Your child understands that volume is how much space a 3D shape takes up and can use unit cubes to measure volume in cubic units.

subject: Math
description: In addition to Proficient, the Advanced student is able to explain how to find missing dimension(s) when given volume and justify.
description_plain: Your child can explain how to find missing measurements of a 3D shape when given its volume and can explain why the method works.

subject: Math
description: Recognize volume as an attribute of three-dimensional figures and understand concepts of volume measurement such as "unit cube" and a volume of n cubic units.
description_plain: Your child understands that volume measures the space inside 3D shapes and can use unit cubes to find volume in cubic units.

subject: Math
description: Geometric measurement: understand concepts of volume and relate volume to multiplication and to addition.
description_plain: Your child understands volume and knows how to find it by multiplying and adding measurements.

subject: Math
description:
The Below Basic student may be able to:
  • Make a line plot to display a data set of measurements in fractions of a unit (½, ¼, 1/8) with partial success. OR
  • Use operations on fractions to solve problems involving information presented in line plots with partial success.
description_plain: Your child may be able to make simple line plots with fractional measurements or use fractions to solve problems from line plots, but might need some help.

subject: Math
description:
The Basic student is able to:
  • Make a line plot to display a data set of measurements in fractions of a unit (½, ¼, 1/8). OR
  • Use operations on fractions to solve problems involving information presented in line plots.
description_plain: Your child can make line plots with fractional measurements and use fractions to solve problems based on the data.

subject: Math
description: Make a line plot to display a data set of measurements in fractions of a unit (½, ¼, 1/8). Use operations on fractions to solve problems involving information presented in line plots.
description_plain: Your child can create line plots showing measurements with fractions and use fraction math to solve problems from the plots.

subject: Math
description:
The Advanced student is able to:
  • Predict ways in which operations with fractions, and fractions on a line plot, would change if the data set were changed. AND
  • Use three or more operations with fractions to solve problems involving information presented in line plots.

subject: Math
description: Recognize a statistical question as one that anticipates variability in the data related to the question and accounts for it in the answers.
description_plain: Your child understands that statistical questions expect different answers because data can vary.

subject: Math
description: Develop understanding of statistical variability.
description_plain: Your child is learning how data can vary and what that means.

subject: Math
description: May be able to complete a table from a given equation.
description_plain: Your child may be able to fill in a table using a given math equation.

subject: Math
description: Describe the relationship between dependent and independent variables from a table or graph.
description_plain: Your child can explain how one quantity changes when another quantity changes by looking at a table or graph.

subject: Math
description: Use variables to represent two quantities in a real-world problem that change in relationship to one another; write an equation to express one quantity (dependent variable) in terms of the other quantity (independent variable). Analyze their relationship using graphs and tables, and relate these to the equation.
description_plain: Your child can use letters to show two related amounts in a real problem, write an equation to connect them, and use graphs and tables to understand their relationship.

subject: Math
description: Create a real-world problem from a given table, graph, or equation and justify the relationship between the dependent and independent variables.
description_plain: Your child can make up a real problem from a table, graph, or equation and explain how the two quantities are related.

subject: Math
description: Use variables to represent two quantities in a real-world problem that change in relationship to one another; write an equation to express one quantity (dependent variable), in terms of the other quantity (independent variable). Analyze their relationship using graphs and tables, and relate these to the equation.
description_plain: Your child can use letters to show two related amounts in a real problem, write an equation to connect them, and use graphs and tables to understand their relationship.

subject: Math
description: Represent and analyze quantitative relationships between dependent and independent variables.
description_plain: Your child can show and study how one quantity depends on another.

subject: Math
description: May be able to recognize the value on an inequality of the form x > c or x < c (with the variable on the left) on number line diagrams.
description_plain: Your child can recognize where a number fits on a number line when given an inequality like x > c or x < c with the variable on the left.

subject: Math
description: Write an inequality of the form x > c or x < c (with the variable on the left) to represent a constraint or condition in a real-world or mathematical problem. Represent solutions by graphing on a number line.
description_plain: Your child can write inequalities like x > c or x < c to show limits in real-life or math problems and draw the solutions on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Write an inequality of the form x > c or x < c (with the variable on either side) to represent a constraint or condition in a real-world or mathematical problem. Recognize that inequalities have infinitely many solutions and represent solutions by graphing on a number line.
description_plain: Your child can write inequalities with the variable on either side to show limits in problems, understand there are many solutions, and graph these on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Write an inequality of the form x ≥ c or x ≤ c (with the variable on either side) to represent a constraint or condition in a real-world or mathematical problem. Recognize that inequalities have infinitely many solutions and represent solutions by graphing on a number line.
description_plain: Your child can write inequalities using ≥ or ≤ with the variable on either side to show limits in problems, know there are many solutions, and show these on a number line.

subject: Math
description: Write an inequality of the form x > c or x < c to represent a constraint or condition in a real-world or mathematical problem. Recognize that inequalities of the form x > c or x < c have infinitely many solutions; represent solutions of such inequalities on number line diagrams.
description_plain: Your child can write inequalities like x > c or x < c to describe limits in problems, understand these have many solutions, and show the answers on a number line.

subject: Math
description: May be able to solve equations in the form of one-step, linear equations involving whole numbers.
description_plain: Your child can solve simple one-step equations using whole numbers.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems in both real-world and mathematical contexts by writing and solving equations in the form of one-step, linear equations involving whole numbers.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life and math problems by writing and solving one-step equations with whole numbers.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems in both real-world and mathematical contexts by writing and solving equations in the form of one-step, linear equations involving nonnegative rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life and math problems by writing and solving one-step equations with nonnegative fractions or decimals.

subject: Math
description: Solve problems in both real-world and mathematical contexts by writing and solving equations in the form of two-step, linear equations involving nonnegative rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child can solve real-life and math problems by writing and solving two-step equations with nonnegative fractions or decimals.

subject: Math
description: Write and solve real-world and mathematical problems in the form of one-step, linear equations involving nonnegative rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child can write and solve one-step equations with nonnegative fractions or decimals to solve real-life and math problems.

subject: Math
description: May be able to identify a variable representing an unknown number.
description_plain: Your child can identify a letter or symbol that stands for a number they don’t know yet.

subject: Math
description: Use variables to represent unknown numbers and write one-step expressions to represent real-world or mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can use letters to stand for unknown numbers and write simple expressions to show real-life or math problems.

subject: Math
description: Use variables to represent unknown numbers and write expressions to represent real-world or mathematical problems.
description_plain: Your child can use letters to stand for unknown numbers and write expressions to represent real-life or math problems.

subject: Math
description: Use variables to represent unknown numbers and write multiple expressions to represent the same real-world or mathematical problem and justify that they are equivalent.
description_plain: Your child can write different expressions using letters for the same problem and explain why they mean the same thing.

subject: Math
description: Use variables to represent unknown numbers and write expressions when solving a real-world or mathematical problem.
description_plain: Your child can use letters to stand for unknown numbers and write expressions when solving real-life or math problems.

subject: Math
description: May be able to use substitution to determine whether a given whole number in a specified set makes a one-step equation true.
description_plain: Your child can check if a whole number makes a simple one-step equation true by plugging it in.

subject: Math
description: Use substitution to determine whether a given whole number in a specified set makes a one-step equation or a one-step inequality true.
description_plain: Your child can check if a whole number makes a one-step equation or inequality true by plugging it in.

subject: Math
description: Use substitution to determine whether a given non-negative rational number in a specified set makes a one-step equation or a one-step inequality true.
description_plain: Your child can check if a nonnegative fraction or decimal makes a one-step equation or inequality true by plugging it in.

subject: Math
description: Use substitution to determine whether a given rational number in a specified set makes a two-step equation or a two-step inequality true.
description_plain: Your child can check if a fraction or decimal makes a two-step equation or inequality true by plugging it in.

subject: Math
description: Understand a solution to an equation or an inequality makes the equation or inequality true. Use substitution to determine whether a given number in a specified set makes an equation or inequality true.
description_plain: Your child understands that a solution makes an equation or inequality true and can check if a number does by plugging it in.

subject: Math
description: Reason about and solve one-variable equations and inequalities.
description_plain: Your child can solve math problems that have one variable, like finding the value of x in an equation or inequality.

subject: Math
description: May be able to identify when two numerical expressions are equivalent.
description_plain: Your child may be able to tell when two number expressions have the same value.

subject: Math
description: Identify a model that matches an expression.
description_plain: Your child can match a math expression to a model or picture that shows what it means.

subject: Math
description: Identify when two expressions are equivalent with positive rational numbers.
description_plain: Your child can recognize when two expressions with positive numbers mean the same thing.

subject: Math
description: Generate equivalent expressions.
description_plain: Your child can create different expressions that have the same value.

subject: Math
description: Identify when two expressions are equivalent.
description_plain: Your child can tell when two math expressions are equal.

subject: Math
description: May be able to identify the properties of operations in equivalent expressions (Commutative Property, Associative Property, Distributive Property).
description_plain: Your child may be able to name math rules used in expressions that are equal, like switching numbers or grouping them.

subject: Math
description: Identify the properties of operations in equivalent expressions (Commutative Property, Associative Property, Distributive Property, Additive Identity Property, Multiplicative Identity Property, and Zero Product Property).
description_plain: Your child can identify math rules like switching order, grouping, distributing, and special numbers in equal expressions.

subject: Math
description: Apply the properties of operations to generate equivalent expressions (Commutative Property, Associative Property, Distributive Property, Additive Identity Property, Multiplicative Identity Property, and Zero Product Property).
description_plain: Your child can use math rules to make new expressions that have the same value.

subject: Math
description: Justify why two expressions are equivalent using the properties of operations.
description_plain: Your child can explain why two expressions are equal by using math rules.

subject: Math
description: Apply the properties of operations to generate equivalent expressions.
description_plain: Your child can use math rules to create expressions that are equal.

subject: Math
description: Use Order of Operations to evaluate algebraic expressions using whole numbers.
description_plain: Your child can solve math expressions with whole numbers by following the correct order of steps.

subject: Math
description: Identify the number of terms in a mathematical expression.
description_plain: Your child can count how many parts (terms) are in a math expression.

subject: Math
description: Identify an algebraic expression.
description_plain: Your child can recognize an algebraic expression, which uses letters and numbers.

subject: Math
description: May be able to write, read, and evaluate expressions in which letters stand for numbers.
description_plain: Your child may be able to write, read, and solve math expressions that use letters to stand for numbers.

subject: Math
description: Use Order of Operations to evaluate algebraic expressions using whole numbers and whole-number exponents. Include expressions that arise from formulas relative to sixth grade geometry standards in real-world problems.
description_plain: Your child can solve math expressions with whole numbers and exponents by following the correct steps, including problems from sixth grade geometry.

subject: Math
description: Identify a part of an expression using mathematical terms (sum, difference, term, product, factor, quotient, coefficient, constant).
description_plain: Your child can name parts of a math expression, like sum, difference, term, product, factor, quotient, coefficient, and constant.

subject: Math
description: Write one-step algebraic expressions.
description_plain: Your child can write simple one-step algebra expressions.

subject: Math
description: Write, read, and evaluate expressions in which letters stand for numbers.
description_plain: Your child can write, read, and solve expressions where letters stand for numbers.

subject: Math
description: Use Order of Operations to evaluate algebraic expressions using positive rational numbers and whole-number exponents. Include expressions that arise from formulas relative to sixth grade standards in real-world problems.
description_plain: Your child can solve math expressions with positive numbers and exponents by following the correct steps, including real-world problems from sixth grade.

subject: Math
description: Identify parts of an expression using mathematical terms (sum, difference, term, product, factor, quotient, coefficient, constant).
description_plain: Your child can name parts of a math expression using words like sum, difference, product, factor, and coefficient.

subject: Math
description: Write two-step algebraic expressions.
description_plain: Your child can write algebra expressions that have two steps.

subject: Math
description: Write, read, and evaluate expressions in which letters stand for numbers.
description_plain: Your child can write, read, and solve math expressions where letters stand for numbers.

subject: Math
description: Use Order of Operations to justify the evaluation of algebraic expressions that contain positive rational numbers and whole-number exponents. Include expressions that arise from formulas relative to sixth grade standards in real-world problems.
description_plain: Your child can use the order of operations to solve expressions with positive fractions and whole-number exponents, including real-life problems.

subject: Math
description: Create an expression given mathematical terms (sum, difference, term, product, factor, quotient, coefficient, constant).
description_plain: Your child can make math expressions when given terms like sum, difference, product, and coefficient.

subject: Math
description: Write algebraic expressions using grouping symbols.
description_plain: Your child can write algebra expressions using parentheses or other grouping symbols.

subject: Math
description: Write, read, and evaluate expressions in which letters stand for numbers.
description_plain: Your child can write, read, and solve expressions where letters represent numbers.

subject: Math
description: Use Order of Operations to evaluate algebraic expressions using positive rational numbers and whole-number exponents. Include expressions that arise from formulas in real-world problems.
description_plain: Your child can use the order of operations to solve expressions with positive fractions and whole-number exponents, including those from real-world formulas.

subject: Math
description: Identify parts of an expression using mathematical terms (sum, difference, term, product, factor, quotient, coefficient, constant).
description_plain: Your child can identify parts of a math expression using words like sum, difference, product, factor, and coefficient.

description_plain: Your child can write expressions that show math operations using numbers and letters for numbers.

subject: Math
description: Write, read, and evaluate expressions in which letters stand for numbers.
description_plain: Your child can write, read, and solve expressions where letters stand for numbers.

subject: Math
description: May be able to write and evaluate one-step numerical expressions involving one whole number exponent.
description_plain: Your child may be able to write and solve one-step math expressions with one whole-number exponent.

subject: Math
description: Write and evaluate two-step numerical expressions involving one whole number exponent.
description_plain: Your child can write and solve two-step math expressions that include one whole-number exponent.

subject: Math
description: Write and evaluate multi-step numerical expressions involving whole number exponents.
description_plain: Your child can write and solve multi-step math expressions that use whole-number exponents.

subject: Math

description_plain: Your child can write and solve multi-step math expressions using the Distributive Property and whole-number exponents.

subject: Math
description: Write and evaluate numerical expressions involving whole-number exponents.

subject: Math

description_plain: Your child can use what they know about arithmetic to work with algebra expressions.

subject: Math
description: May be able to solve real-world and mathematical problems by graphing points in quadrant one of the coordinate plane. Find distances between points with the same first coordinate or the same second coordinate.

description_plain: Your child can solve problems by plotting points in all four quadrants of a coordinate plane and find distances between points with the same x or y value in the same quadrant.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems by graphing points in all four quadrants of the coordinate plane. Find distances between points with the same first coordinate or the same second coordinate; relate absolute value and distance.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems by graphing non-integer points in all four quadrants of the coordinate plane. Find distances between non-integer points with the same first coordinate or the same second coordinate; relate absolute value and distance.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems by plotting points with decimals in all four parts of a graph and find distances between points that share the same x or y value, understanding how absolute value relates to distance.

subject: Math
description: Solve real-world and mathematical problems by graphing points in all four quadrants of the coordinate plane. Find distances between points with the same first coordinate or the same second coordinate; relate absolute value and distance.
description_plain: Your child can solve problems by plotting points in all four parts of a graph and find distances between points that share the same x or y value, connecting absolute value to distance.

subject: Math
description: Distinguish comparisons of absolute value from statements about order.
description_plain: Your child can tell the difference between comparing absolute values and comparing which numbers are bigger or smaller.

subject: Math
description: Describe the absolute value of a whole number as its distance from 0 on the number line; interpret absolute value as magnitude for a positive quantity in a real-world situation.
description_plain: Your child understands that absolute value means how far a whole number is from zero on a number line and can use this to explain positive amounts in real life.

description: Write, interpret, and explain statements of order for whole numbers in real-world contexts.

subject: Math

description_plain: Your child understands that inequality statements show where two numbers are located compared to each other on a number line.

description_plain: Your child understands that absolute value shows how far a rational number is from zero on a number line and can explain this for positive or negative amounts in real life.

description: Write, interpret, and explain statements of order for rational numbers in real-world contexts.
description_plain: Your child can write and explain how rational numbers are ordered in real-life situations.

subject: Math
description: Create statements of inequality about the relative position of two numbers on a (vertical or horizontal) number line diagram.

description_plain: Your child can order rational numbers, including percentages, and understand their absolute values.

description: Distinguish comparisons of absolute value from statements about order.
description_plain: Your child understands the difference between comparing absolute values and comparing which number is greater or smaller.

description: Understand the absolute value of a rational number as its distance from 0 on the number line; interpret absolute value as magnitude for a positive or negative quantity in a real-world situation.
description_plain: Your child understands absolute value as how far a number is from zero on a number line and can explain this in real-life situations.

description: Write, interpret, and explain statements of order for rational numbers in real-world contexts.
description_plain: Your child can write and explain which rational numbers come before or after others in real-world examples.
